💭 Рефлексия: Можно ли использовать 'Were... to' для вполне вероятного события — или эта конструкция только для маловероятных сценариев? Как это влияет на выбор в академическом тексте?

📙 Grammar 2 · Counterfactual Past: продвинутый Type 3

Had it not been for · Had + subject + past participle · But for · Only if · Even if
🟣

Type 3 в базовом варианте: 'If they had regulated AI earlier, fewer harms would have occurred.'

В уровне C мы изучаем инвертированные и сокращённые формы прошедшего контрфактического, а также специальные конструкции, которые выражают контрфактические отношения без явного 'if'.
Конструкция Формула Значение Пример
Inverted Type 3 Had + subject + past participle... , would/could/might + have + pp Гипотетическое прошлое — формальный вариант Had the algorithmic bias been identified at the design stage, the discriminatory outcomes could have been prevented.
Had it not been for Had it not been for + NP... , would/could/might + have + pp Без X — Y бы не произошло Had it not been for the whistleblower's disclosure, the extent of the data misuse would have remained concealed from the public.
But for But for + NP... , would/could/might + (have) + pp Формальный эквивалент 'Had it not been for' But for the intervention of independent researchers, the flawed training data would have continued to produce biased outputs indefinitely.
Even if (counterfactual) Even if + past perfect... , would still + have + pp Контрфактическое уступительное: Y верно несмотря на X Even if the developers had implemented standard bias-detection protocols, the underlying data distribution would still have produced systematically skewed results.
Only if (counterfactual) Only if + past perfect... , would + have + pp (inverted) Единственное достаточное условие в прошлом Only if mandatory algorithmic audits had been introduced before deployment would the scale of the problem have become apparent.

📐 Контрфактическое рассуждение как аргументативный метод

Контрфактическое предложение — это не просто грамматика. Это аналитический инструмент, который позволяет:

1. Установить причинность: 'Had X not occurred, Y would not have followed' → X была необходимой причиной Y.

2. Оценить альтернативы: 'Had policy P been adopted, outcome O would have been avoided' → P была жизнеспособной альтернативой.

3. Показать необратимость: 'Even if X had been done, Y would still have occurred' → X не была достаточной мерой.

4. Выделить единственный выход: 'Only if X had happened would Y have been prevented' → X была единственным достаточным условием.

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

Had the major technology companies operated under a binding international regulatory framework from the outset — rather than in the largely ungoverned digital commons that actually prevailed — the accumulation of monopolistic data advantage that now renders meaningful competition structurally impossible would, in all likelihood, have been substantially curtailed.

① Had the major technology companies operated under — inverted Type 3: formal counterfactual; no 'if' required

② rather than in the largely ungoverned digital commons that actually prevailed — embedded contrast: 'actually prevailed' подчёркивает реальность как контраст к гипотезе

③ in all likelihood — epistemic adverbial phrase: хеджинг counterfactual consequence

④ would... have been substantially curtailed — passive perfect in consequence clause + degree adverb
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Had they would have regulated AI, problems wouldn't happen.

Правильно: Had they implemented robust AI regulation at the outset, many of the harms that have since materialised would have been preventable.

Почему: Никогда не используй 'would have' в if/had-клаузе. 'Had' — это уже прошедший subjunctive; consequence = 'would + have + pp'

Ошибка: But for the lack of regulation, AI became dangerous.

Правильно: But for the absence of adequate regulatory oversight, the current landscape of unchecked algorithmic deployment would not have emerged.

Почему: 'But for' требует условного следствия ('would have'), а не простого прошедшего
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Counterfactual Past

✔ 'Had it not been for + NP' → элегантная замена длинному 'If it had not been for'; LR Band 8

✔ 'But for' в академическом анализе причинности → редкая конструкция, сигнал зрелого письма (GRA)

✔ 'Even if + past perfect... would still have' → логика необходимости vs достаточности = сильный аналитический сигнал (TA)
⚠ Снижает балл: 'Would have' в if/had-клаузе — самая частая ошибка в counterfactual; немедленно снижает GRA
📝 Задание 2 · Counterfactual Past

1. Трансформируйте в инвертированную форму: (a) 'If the researchers had detected the bias earlier, the algorithm would not have been deployed.' (b) 'If there had not been public pressure, the company would not have changed its data policy.' (c) 'If the EU AI Act had been implemented five years earlier, significant harms would have been prevented.'

2. Используйте контрфактическое рассуждение для каждой аналитической задачи: (d) Установите причинность — почему Cambridge Analytica стал возможен (Had it not been for...). (e) Покажите недостаточность меры — почему публичное раскрытие алгоритмов не решило бы проблему (Even if... would still have).

3. Напишите абзац (70–80 слов) об истории регулирования ИИ, используя минимум 2 разных контрфактических конструкции.

💭 Рефлексия: Как контрфактическое рассуждение отличается от обычного аргумента? Что именно оно доказывает — и что не может доказать?

📗 Grammar 3 · As if / As though / As were · Imagining a world where

Сравнительные и гипотетические конструкции для аналитического воображения
🟣

'As if' и 'as though' — это не просто сравнение.

В уровне C они становятся инструментами риторического и аналитического воображения: автор создаёт иллюзию реальности для сценария, который ещё не существует — или никогда не существовал.
Конструкция Временная логика Академическая функция Пример
as if / as though + past simple Нереальное настоящее Описание текущей ситуации как будто что-то нереальное истинно Technology companies currently operate as if the social consequences of their products were entirely external to their domain of responsibility.
as if / as though + past perfect Нереальное прошлое Критика текущего состояния через прошлое нереальное Regulators have responded to algorithmic harm as if the framework developed for broadcast media were directly applicable to decentralised digital platforms.
as it were Метафорическая оговорка Смягчает нестандартное употребление слова или метафору The algorithm functions as a hidden mediator, as it were, between producer intent and consumer choice.
Imagine / Imagine a world where Риторический приём Создаёт гипотетический сценарий для проверки принципа Imagine a world in which every decision affecting a citizen's access to credit, healthcare, or employment were made by an opaque algorithmic system — what democratic accountability mechanisms would remain meaningful?
Think of a scenario where Менее риторический, более аналитический Вводит конкретный мысленный эксперимент Think of a scenario where a self-driving vehicle must choose between two equally harmful outcomes: existing legal frameworks offer no clear resolution.

⚡ As if + past vs as if + past perfect: временная точность

As if + past simple — нереальное настоящее:

'Companies behave as if regulation were irrelevant.'

→ В настоящий момент они ведут себя так, как будто (но это не так сейчас)

As if + past perfect — нереальное прошлое:

'The company responded as if no warnings had ever been issued.'

→ Их реакция в прошлом выглядела так, как будто (но предупреждения были)

⚠ В формальном академическом тексте 'as if + were' (subjunctive) предпочтительнее 'as if + was'.

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

Contemporary AI governance frameworks are designed as if the fundamental questions of liability, transparency, and accountability had already been resolved — as if, that is, the regulatory categories developed for human agents and corporate entities could be seamlessly mapped onto systems whose decision-making processes are, by design, opaque even to their creators.

① are designed as if... had already been resolved — as if + past perfect: критика текущей политики через нереальное прошлое

② as if, that is — parenthetical reformulation (C13) встроен внутрь второго 'as if': двойная гипотеза

③ the regulatory categories developed for human agents — reduced participial post-modifier внутри hypothetical clause

④ whose decision-making processes are, by design, opaque even to their creators — sentence adverb 'by design' (C13) + relative clause
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Companies act as if they don't have responsibility for AI harms.

Правильно: Technology companies operate as if the social consequences of their systems were entirely external to their domain of accountability.

Почему: После 'as if' в нереальном настоящем — past subjunctive ('were', не 'don't have'). Subjunctive 'were' работает для всех лиц

Ошибка: Imagine a world where AI makes all decisions and what would happen.

Правильно: Imagine a world in which all consequential civic decisions were delegated to algorithmic systems — what democratic accountability structures would remain functional?

Почему: 'Imagine a world where' требует subordinate clause + риторический вопрос или следствие. Нельзя просто добавить 'and what would happen' — это не завершённая структура
🔵 Examiner's Eye — As if / Imagining

✔ 'As if + past perfect' для критики текущей политики → аналитическая элегантность без прямого обвинения (TA + LR)

✔ 'Imagine a world in which... were... — what would remain?' → риторический мысленный эксперимент = Band 8 TA

✔ 'As it were' для метафоры → показывает осознанность нестандартного употребления (GRA + LR)
⚠ Снижает балл: 'As if + will/would' — грамматически невозможно в нереальном условном; 'as if + present simple' используется только в реальном сравнении
📝 Задание 3 · As if / As though / Imagining

1. Трансформируйте в 'as if' + нужное время: (a) 'Tech companies ignore the social cost of AI — but there is one.' → 'Tech companies operate as if...' (b) 'Regulators treated AI as if it were the same as previous technologies — but it is not.' → полная форма с as if + past perfect.

2. Напишите мысленный эксперимент (2–3 предложения) об ИИ, используя 'Imagine a world in which... were...' + следствие в виде риторического вопроса.

3. Найдите одно предложение из вашего предыдущего эссе, где вы делаете прямое утверждение об опасности ИИ. Перепишите его как 'as if'-конструкцию. Сравните: что убедительнее и почему?

💭 Рефлексия: Почему 'Imagine a world where X' делает аргумент более убедительным, чем 'X would be dangerous'? В чём психологическая разница?

📕 Grammar 4 · Интеграция и Band 8 Upgrade

Контрфактическое мышление как аргументативная архитектура: от предположения к анализу
🟣

Теперь ты знаешь три инструмента гипотетического мышления.

В Grammar 4 мы видим, как они работают вместе в аргументативном эссе: гипотетический сценарий → контрфактический анализ → аналитическое воображение — как три шага одного интеллектуального движения.
Аргументативная задача Инструмент Пример Логическая функция
Показать риск будущего Were + to / Should Were AI to be granted legal personhood, the entire architecture of human rights law would require reconstruction. Конкретизирует абстрактный риск через сценарий
Установить историческую причину Had... / But for But for the absence of binding international AI standards, the proliferation of surveillance capitalism would have been substantially constrained. Доказывает причинность через её отсутствие
Показать недостаточность меры Even if... would still Even if algorithmic transparency were mandated, users would still lack the technical literacy to evaluate the outputs they receive. Опровергает предложенное решение логически
Критиковать текущую ситуацию As if + past subjunctive Policymakers continue to regulate AI as if it were simply a more efficient version of existing technologies. Разоблачает ложную аналогию
Создать мысленный эксперимент Imagine a world where Imagine a world in which all hiring decisions were made by algorithms with no human review — what notion of procedural justice would remain coherent? Приглашает читателя почувствовать последствия

📐 Band 8 Counterfactual Paragraph — полная архитектура

Тезис (прямое утверждение): The absence of binding international AI governance frameworks represents one of the most consequential policy failures of the current era.

Контрфактический анализ (Had... / But for): Had such frameworks been established during the formative years of the commercial internet — when the infrastructural dependencies that now make retroactive regulation so difficult were still being formed — the current concentration of algorithmic power in a small number of private entities would have been substantially less likely to emerge.

Текущая критика (as if): Instead, regulators have responded as if each successive technological platform were a novel and unprecedented phenomenon, rather than a variation upon a recurring structural pattern of market concentration and regulatory arbitrage.

Мысленный эксперимент (Imagine): Were this pattern to continue unchecked, one might reasonably ask: what remains of democratic accountability in a world in which the infrastructural conditions of public life are determined by systems whose objectives are opaque, whose operators are extraterritorial, and whose consequences are irreversible?
🚀 Band 8 Upgrade

Band 6: AI is dangerous if not regulated. If we don't regulate it, bad things will happen.

Band 7: Without adequate regulation, AI systems may produce harmful outcomes that prove difficult to reverse. Had stronger governance frameworks been in place earlier, some of these risks might have been mitigated.

Band 8: Were autonomous AI systems to be deployed at scale without binding international governance frameworks — a scenario that, it must be acknowledged, falls within the bounds of current technical and commercial trajectory — the consequences for epistemic autonomy, labour market stability, and democratic accountability would, had the formative window for preventive regulation now passed, be considerably more difficult to address than proponents of a permissive approach have, thus far, been willing to acknowledge.
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Full Counterfactual Integration

✔ Were... to + Had... + as if + Imagine в одном абзаце → системное владение гипотетическим мышлением = Band 8 TA + GRA

✔ Counterfactual как аналитический метод (причинность, достаточность, альтернатива) → высший уровень TA

✔ Смешение с C9 (fronting), C12 (complex NP), C13 (comment clauses) → синтез уровня C = Band 8 GRA
⚠ Снижает балл: Гипотетический сценарий без аналитического вывода — это не аргумент; всегда показывай, что следует из мысленного эксперимента
📝 Задание 4 · Full Counterfactual Integration

1. Напишите полный аргументативный абзац (90–100 слов) об ИИ и регулировании, используя схему из Grammar 4: тезис → Had... (причинность) → as if... (текущая критика) → Were... to... (будущий риск).

2. Перепишите следующий слабый абзац, добавив контрфактическое мышление: 'AI can be biased. This is a problem. Companies should fix this. If they don't, there will be issues. We need regulation.'

3. Проверьте свой абзац: (a) Каждая контрфактическая конструкция грамматически точна? (b) Есть ли аналитический вывод из каждого мысленного эксперимента? (c) Гипотеза обоснована — не просто фантастика?

💭 Рефлексия: Прочитайте любую академическую статью об этике ИИ. Найдите контрфактическое или гипотетическое предложение. Какую аналитическую функцию оно выполняет в аргументе?

📖 Reading 1 · The Alignment Problem

Академическое чтение: контрфактическое мышление в ИИ-дискурсе
📖 The Alignment Problem: Hypothetical Risks, Counterfactual Reasoning, and the Ethics of Precaution

It is worth noting, at the outset, that debates surrounding the long-term risks of advanced artificial intelligence are, in a distinctive sense, inherently counterfactual: they concern not what has happened but what might happen, under conditions that do not yet obtain. Were a system with genuinely general cognitive capabilities to emerge — a development that leading researchers regard as, at minimum, a non-negligible possibility within the coming decades — the question of whether its objectives would remain aligned with human values cannot, it would appear, be resolved by extrapolation from the behaviour of current systems.

Had the developers of early social media platforms modelled the long-term societal consequences of engagement-maximising recommendation algorithms — rather than treating them as if they were simply neutral conduits for user preference — the epistemic and democratic harms that have since materialised might, at least in part, have been anticipated and constrained by design. This counterfactual observation does not, admittedly, demonstrate that similar foresight would have proved sufficient to prevent the harms in question. Even if algorithmic transparency had been mandated from the outset, users would still have lacked the technical and critical literacy required to exercise meaningful oversight over systems whose complexity was, by design, irreducible.

What this analysis suggests, I would contend, is that the precautionary logic applicable to AI development is not one of prediction but of structured imagination: the capacity to construct detailed, technically grounded counterfactual scenarios and to ask, systematically, what governance mechanisms would have been necessary to produce different outcomes. Imagine a world in which such imaginative rigour had been applied to social media in 2008 — it is, tellingly, far from obvious that the institutional preconditions for such application existed. The question of whether they exist, or can be created, for AI is the defining governance challenge of the present moment.

📝 Задания к Reading 1 — Анализ гипотетических конструкций

1. В первом абзаце: 'Were a system with genuinely general cognitive capabilities to emerge...' Разберите грамматику: тип инверсии, почему 'to emerge', как обоснована гипотеза через appositive NP-хеджинг.

2. Второй абзац содержит 'Had the developers... modelled... rather than treating them as if they were...' — это сложная комбинация: inverted Type 3 + embedded as if + past subjunctive. Разберите все три слоя. Какую аналитическую функцию выполняет каждый?

3. 'Even if algorithmic transparency had been mandated... users would still have lacked...' — это контрфактическое уступительное. Объясните логику: что именно это предложение доказывает относительно прозрачности как меры? (Подсказка: достаточность vs необходимость)

4. 'Imagine a world in which such imaginative rigour had been applied to social media in 2008' — автор использует 'had been applied' (прошедшее). Почему past perfect внутри 'imagine a world in which'? Какое время было бы в нереальном настоящем сценарии?

5. Напишите продолжение текста (80–90 слов) в виде четвёртого абзаца, используя: 'Were such institutional preconditions to be established...' (будущий сценарий) + 'Had earlier regulatory windows not been missed...' (прошедший контрфактический) + заключение с 'What this suggests is...'

📚 Словарь урока — C1–C2 Academic Vocabulary (30 слов)

Слово Значение / Коллокация Пример
alignment (AI) согласованность целей; alignment problem / value alignment / misalignment The alignment problem asks whether AI objectives can be made reliably consistent with human values.
autonomous автономный; autonomous system / autonomous decision-making Autonomous weapons systems raise profound questions about accountability and proportionality.
opacity непрозрачность; algorithmic opacity / structural opacity The opacity of deep learning systems makes independent auditing extremely difficult.
accountability подотчётность; algorithmic accountability / lack of accountability Meaningful accountability requires that decision-makers be identifiable and their reasoning examinable.
extrapolation экстраполяция; extrapolation from data / beyond extrapolation Long-term AI risk cannot be resolved by extrapolation from current system behaviour.
precautionary предупредительный; precautionary principle / precautionary logic The precautionary principle holds that absence of certainty is not grounds for regulatory inaction.
proliferation распространение; proliferation of AI / rapid proliferation The proliferation of facial recognition technology has outpaced democratic oversight.
trajectory траектория; technological trajectory / current trajectory The current commercial trajectory of AI development prioritises capability over safety.
irreversible необратимый; irreversible harm / irreversible consequences Some AI-driven social changes may prove irreversible once they become structurally embedded.
extraterritorial экстерриториальный; extraterritorial jurisdiction / extraterritorial platform Extraterritorial platforms challenge the traditional model of national regulatory jurisdiction.
surveillance слежка; surveillance capitalism / mass surveillance / algorithmic surveillance Surveillance capitalism monetises behavioural data at a scale unprecedented in human history.
epistemic эпистемологический; epistemic autonomy / epistemic harm / epistemic inequality Algorithmic filter bubbles threaten epistemic autonomy by restricting exposure to diverse viewpoints.
liability ответственность (юридическая); AI liability / establish liability Existing liability frameworks are ill-equipped to assign responsibility for AI-caused harms.
arbitrage арбитраж; regulatory arbitrage / jurisdictional arbitrage Firms engage in regulatory arbitrage by locating operations in less restrictive jurisdictions.
retroactive ретроактивный, задним числом; retroactive regulation / retroactive accountability Retroactive regulation of AI may prove insufficient once harmful applications are already entrenched.
opaque непрозрачный; opaque algorithm / opaque decision-making Opaque algorithmic systems challenge the transparency requirements of due process.
cognitive когнитивный; cognitive capability / cognitive bias / cognitive load General cognitive capability — the ability to learn and reason across domains — remains an unsolved research challenge.
cascading каскадный; cascading failure / cascading consequences A single misaligned AI decision in critical infrastructure could produce cascading failures across interconnected systems.
mandate (v) обязывать, предписывать; mandate transparency / mandate audits The proposed regulation would mandate independent algorithmic audits before deployment.
constrain ограничивать; constrain development / constrain by design Governance frameworks aim to constrain the most harmful applications of AI without stifling innovation.
deploy развёртывать, внедрять; deploy AI / deploy at scale Deploying AI systems at scale without adequate testing has produced foreseeable harms.
foreseeable предсказуемый; foreseeable harm / reasonably foreseeable Liability may attach to AI developers for reasonably foreseeable harms resulting from system misuse.
non-negligible незначительный в отрицательном смысле; non-negligible risk / non-negligible probability Researchers assign a non-negligible probability to transformative AI within the current century.
structural структурный; structural pattern / structural inequality / structural precondition Market concentration in AI is a structural outcome, not the product of individual bad actors.
rigour строгость, тщательность; analytical rigour / empirical rigour Counterfactual reasoning requires the same analytical rigour as empirical evidence.
entrenched укоренившийся; deeply entrenched / become entrenched Once entrenched in infrastructure, harmful AI practices become extremely difficult to reverse.
conduit канал, посредник; neutral conduit / conduit for information Platforms claimed to be neutral conduits for user content while actively shaping it algorithmically.
mitigation смягчение; risk mitigation / harm mitigation Mitigation strategies must address both the probability and the magnitude of potential AI harms.
scrutiny тщательное изучение; public scrutiny / regulatory scrutiny / withstand scrutiny Algorithmic decision systems should be subject to the same public scrutiny as human institutions.
asymmetry асимметрия; information asymmetry / power asymmetry The information asymmetry between AI developers and regulators impedes effective oversight.

📖 Reading 2 · Governing the Ungovernable

Контрфактическое рассуждение в нормативном аргументе
📖 Governing the Ungovernable: Counterfactual Scenarios and the Ethics of AI Precaution

The governance of artificial intelligence confronts a structural paradox: the pace of technological development renders anticipatory regulation technically difficult, yet the irreversibility of many potential AI-driven social transformations makes retroactive regulation politically and practically insufficient. Had the institutional responses to earlier transformative technologies — most notably the commercial internet — been designed with a more rigorous counterfactual imagination, the argument runs, the accumulation of unchecked market power and the systematic exploitation of personal data that now characterise the digital economy might have been substantially curtailed. This argument is not, admittedly, without its critics.

Even if such foresight had been available, one might contend, the political conditions necessary to translate anticipatory analysis into binding regulation were, in all likelihood, absent. The regulatory capture that has characterised the relationship between technology firms and governmental bodies would, but for extraordinary political will of the kind rarely available in stable democratic conditions, have obstructed even well-designed precautionary frameworks. As it were, the governance challenge was not primarily epistemic but political: not a failure to imagine consequences but a failure to construct and sustain the coalitions necessary to act upon them.

Were this diagnosis correct — and it is, I would contend, broadly more convincing than purely technocratic accounts — then the appropriate response to the current AI governance challenge is not simply more sophisticated regulation but a more fundamental rethinking of the political conditions under which technology governance is even possible. Imagine a world in which digital infrastructure were treated as a public utility subject to democratic oversight rather than as private property subject only to market discipline — what different institutional landscape might have been inherited, and what different choices might remain available to us now?

📝 Задания к Reading 2 — Нормативный аргумент

1. Второй абзац содержит 'would, but for extraordinary political will... have obstructed'. Разберите эту конструкцию: это 'but for' + Type 3? Или смешанный conditional? Как 'but for' функционирует грамматически и риторически в этом контексте?

2. 'As it were, the governance challenge was not primarily epistemic but political' — здесь 'as it were' используется не как 'as if', а как метафорическая оговорка. Объясните: что именно она смягчает? Что было бы без неё?

3. В третьем абзаце: 'Were this diagnosis correct — and it is, I would contend, broadly more convincing...' — инвертированный Type 2 с встроенным epistemic parenthetical. Автор делает нечто необычное: подтверждает гипотезу прямо внутри условной клаузы. Какой риторический эффект это создаёт?

4. 'Imagine a world in which digital infrastructure were treated as a public utility...' — здесь два вопроса в конце: 'what different institutional landscape might have been inherited' (прошедшее) и 'what different choices might remain available' (настоящее). Это смешанный counterfactual. Объясните временную логику.

5. Напишите четвёртый абзац (80–90 слов) с ответом на риторический вопрос из третьего абзаца, используя: Had digital infrastructure been treated as a public utility... + Were such a model now to be retroactively applied... + What this suggests is that...

✍️ Writing Task 1 · Bar Chart Description

Гипотетическое и контрфактическое мышление в описании данных
✍️ Writing Task 1 · Bar Chart (170 слов, цель: Band 7.5)

Задание:

The bar chart below shows public trust in AI-powered decision-making systems across five sectors (healthcare, criminal justice, financial services, education, and employment) in 2023, measured as the percentage of survey respondents who expressed 'high' or 'very high' confidence in AI-generated decisions. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

📌 Model Opening — Band 8:

Strikingly, public trust in AI decision-making varies considerably across sectors in ways that, it would appear, reflect the perceived stakes and reversibility of the decisions involved rather than any consistent assessment of the underlying technology's reliability. Were the data to be interpreted straightforwardly, one might conclude that healthcare AI commands the greatest public confidence — a finding that is, notably, not easily reconciled with the well-documented opacity of clinical AI systems.

① Strikingly (sentence adverb, C13) + complex NP subject: аналитический комментарий к данным

② it would appear — parenthetical hedging: интерпретация, не утверждение

③ Were the data to be interpreted straightforwardly — inverted Type 2: гипотетическая интерпретация

④ a finding that is, notably, not easily reconciled — appositive NP + sentence adverb в середине predicate

👁 What the Examiner Sees:

TA (7–8): Описана вариация + предложена интерпретация через гипотетическую рамку

CC (7–8): Sentence adverb → comment clause → hypothetical → appositive: четырёхступенчатая прогрессия

LR (7–8): reversibility, reconciled, opacity, perceived stakes — точная академическая лексика

GRA (7–8): Inverted conditional в дескриптивном тексте — редко и убедительно

📐 Структура эссе:

Overview (2 пр.): Strikingly [паттерн]. Were the data to suggest a uniform trend, it would — but it does not.

Body 1 (3–4 пр.): Наибольшее доверие: Had public awareness of AI opacity been higher, trust levels might have differed.

Body 2 (3–4 пр.): Наименьшее доверие: As if the risks in criminal justice were merely technical rather than ethical.

✍️ Writing Task 2 · Advantages / Disadvantages Essay

Гипотетическое мышление в аргументации: преимущества и риски ИИ
✍️ Writing Task 2 · Advantages & Disadvantages Essay (270 слов, цель: Band 7.5)

Задание:

Artificial intelligence is increasingly being used to make important decisions in areas such as healthcare, criminal justice, and employment. What are the advantages and disadvantages of relying on AI for such decisions, and what is your overall assessment?

📌 Model Opening — Band 8:

Were the potential of AI-assisted decision-making to be evaluated solely on the basis of its demonstrable capacity to reduce human cognitive error and scale consistent judgement across large populations, the case for its adoption in high-stakes domains would, arguably, be compelling. The evidence, it would appear, broadly supports the contention that well-designed algorithmic systems can, under controlled conditions, outperform individual human decision-makers on narrowly defined tasks.

① Were the potential... to be evaluated solely on the basis of — inverted Type 2 тезиса: ограничивает условие, при котором аргумент работает

② the case for its adoption... would, arguably, be compelling — epistemic hedging в consequence clause

③ it would appear — parenthetical comment clause (C13): дистанцирование от утверждения о доказательствах

④ under controlled conditions — limiting adverbial: ограничение области применимости (хеджинг C13 уровня 4)

👁 What the Examiner Sees:

TA (7–8): Преимущества и недостатки развёрнуты; позиция выражена через Were... + I would contend

CC (7–8): Inverted conditional в Intro → Had it been → as if → Imagine в Body 1–3: единая гипотетическая архитектура

LR (7–8): cognitive error, high-stakes, algorithmic, procedural justice, opacity — C2 лексика

GRA (7–8): Inverted Type 2 + comment clause + limiting adverbial в двух предложениях

📐 Структура эссе:

Intro (2 пр.): Were... to be evaluated → тезис с 'arguably'. Ограничение сферы применимости.

Advantages (3–4 пр.): Had human decision-making not been subject to bias... / Imagine a world where diagnostic AI were available to all...

Disadvantages (3–4 пр.): Even if algorithmic transparency were mandated... / Systems operate as if procedural justice were irrelevant...

Own Assessment + Conclusion (3 пр.): Were such systems to be governed by binding ethical frameworks... / I would contend... / What this analysis suggests is...

🎓 Урок C14 завершён!

✅ Unreal present/future conditionals: Were + to-inf / Should + inversion — формальные инвертированные формы для гипотетического анализа

✅ Counterfactual past: Had... / But for / Even if... would still / Only if — четыре логических функции (причинность, достаточность, альтернативы, единственное условие)

✅ As if / As though / As it were / Imagine a world where — риторическое и аналитическое воображение

✅ Контрфактическое мышление как метод: не просто грамматика, а аналитический инструмент для установления причинности и оценки альтернатив

✅ Band 8 Counterfactual Paragraph: тезис → Had... → as if... → Were... → What this suggests

💡 Central Question — Ответ:

«Невозможные» предложения делают аргумент реальнее по единственной причине: они конкретизируют абстрактный риск или принцип в воображаемый, но детально прописанный сценарий. Читатель не просто понимает — он представляет. Контрфактическое мышление — это не уклонение от реальности. Это самый точный способ установить, что в реальности было необходимым, что — достаточным, и что — возможным, но упущенным.

▶ Следующий урок — C15: Register Mastery. Register Mastery — formal/neutral/informal continuum. Почему один и тот же смысл в разных регистрах — это разные сообщения?
C15:
A to C

IELTS FROM ZERO

Урок C15 · Upper-Intermediate / Advanced

Register Mastery

Почему один и тот же смысл в разных регистрах — это разные сообщения?

Тематика: Климат и энергетическая политика · Словарь: C1–C2 Academic · Writing: Report + Discursive Essay

💥 Урочный крючок — Зачем это нужно?

Четыре способа сказать одно и то же о климатической политике:

Informal: The government totally messed up on climate — they basically ignored the science for years.

Neutral: The government failed to act on climate change despite clear scientific evidence.

Formal: Successive administrations demonstrably failed to translate the scientific consensus on anthropogenic climate change into commensurate policy action.

Hyper-formal (legal/bureaucratic): It is submitted that the measures adopted by the relevant governmental authorities have, notwithstanding the available evidence base, fallen materially short of the standard required by international obligations.

Смысл один. Но каждый регистр создаёт разные отношения между автором, читателем и предметом. Регистр — это не выбор слов. Это выбор позиции.

📘 Grammar 1 · The Register Continuum: от разговорного к формальному

Lexical, syntactic, and pragmatic dimensions of register — три оси контроля
🟣

Студенты уровня B знают: 'не используй сленг в эссе'.

Студенты уровня C понимают, что регистр — это трёхмерная система: лексика (какие слова), синтаксис (какие структуры), прагматика (какая позиция автора к читателю).

Три оси регистра

Ось 1 — Лексика: выбор между словами разного регистра при одном значении.

Informal → Neutral → Formal: go up → increase → escalate → undergo a sustained upward trajectory

Ось 2 — Синтаксис: выбор структур (активный/пассивный, личный/безличный, простой/сложный).

Informal → Formal: 'We should do something.' → 'It is submitted that concerted action is required.'

Ось 3 — Прагматика: дистанция между автором и читателем; явность/скрытость авторской позиции.

Informal → Formal: 'I think...' → 'It would appear that...' → 'It is widely contended that...'

INFORMAL / SPOKEN

Contractions: it's, don't, won't

Phrasal verbs: cut down, speed up, look into

Vague language: kind of, sort of, things like that

First person direct: I think, we should, in my view

Short sentences, coordination: and, but, so

Emotive language: terrible, amazing, shocking

NEUTRAL / STANDARD

Full forms: it is, do not, will not

One-word verbs: reduce, accelerate, examine

Precise language: significant, considerable, substantial

Hedged first person: it seems, research suggests

Compound sentences with however, therefore

Evaluative but measured: problematic, concerning

FORMAL / ACADEMIC

Nominalisations: reduction, acceleration, examination

Latinate vocabulary: mitigate, attenuate, ameliorate

Impersonal constructions: it is widely acknowledged

Sentence adverbs: crucially, notwithstanding, arguably

Complex NPs with pre/post-modification (C12)

Hedged evaluative: arguably counterproductive, it would appear
Informal / Colloquial Neutral Formal / Academic Function
go up a lot increase significantly undergo a sustained and substantial escalation quantity change
use less energy reduce energy consumption achieve measurable reductions in per-capita energy demand goal description
the government did nothing policymakers failed to act successive administrations demonstrably failed to translate scientific consensus into commensurate policy action critique
it's a big problem this represents a significant challenge this constitutes one of the most consequential governance failures of the current era evaluation
we need to fix this fast urgent action is required it is submitted that the imperative for immediate and substantive policy reform is now incontrovertible recommendation
some people think critics argue it has been contended by a number of prominent scholars attribution
get worse deteriorate undergo progressive and potentially irreversible degradation process
help the environment reduce environmental impact mitigate the adverse environmental consequences of purpose clause

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

The demonstrably inadequate policy response to anthropogenic climate change — a response that successive administrations have, it is widely acknowledged, calibrated to the demands of electoral cycles rather than to the imperatives of physical science — constitutes, notwithstanding the formal commitments undertaken under the Paris Agreement, one of the most consequential governance failures of the contemporary era.

① demonstrably inadequate policy response — formal register: evaluative adverb + Latinate adj + nominalised NP (C12)

② it is widely acknowledged — impersonal passive comment clause (C13): maximum pragmatic distance

③ calibrated to the demands of electoral cycles rather than to the imperatives of physical science — Latinate verb + parallel NP structure (C9 balance)

④ notwithstanding the formal commitments undertaken under — formal concession marker (C11) + reduced participial post-modifier
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Climate change is really bad and we need to do something about it ASAP.

Правильно: The accelerating pace of anthropogenic climate change constitutes an urgent and multidimensional governance challenge that demands substantive and immediate policy reform.

Почему: 'Really bad', 'do something', 'ASAP' — informal markers inappropriate in academic writing. Замени эмотивную лексику на точную номинализованную оценку

Ошибка: In my opinion I personally think that renewable energy is the best solution.

Правильно: It is widely contended that the accelerated transition to renewable energy sources represents the most technically viable pathway to decarbonisation.

Почему: Двойное личное маркирование ('In my opinion I personally') — избыточно в любом регистре. В академическом — замени на impersonal или hedged formal structure
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Register Continuum

✔ Последовательно формальный регистр на протяжении всего эссе → LR Band 7–8: нет смешения уровней

✔ Nominalisations + Latinate vocabulary + impersonal passive → три одновременных маркера формального регистра

✔ Отсутствие contractions, phrasal verbs, vague language → базовая, но необходимая основа Band 6+
⚠ Снижает балл: Смешение регистров (formal sentence → informal phrase → formal again) — хуже, чем последовательный neutral; экзаменатор воспринимает это как потерю контроля над языком
📝 Задание 1 · Register Continuum

1. Трансформируйте в formal/academic регистр по всем трём осям: (a) 'The government is totally failing on green energy.' (b) 'Scientists say things are getting worse fast.' (c) 'We should cut down on fossil fuels before it's too late.' (d) 'A lot of people don't care about climate change.'

2. Определите регистр каждого предложения и объясните три признака, по которым вы его определили: (e) 'Carbon pricing mechanisms have been adopted in an increasing number of jurisdictions.' (f) 'Loads of countries are kind of starting to use carbon taxes.' (g) 'It is submitted that a binding international carbon pricing framework constitutes the most economically efficient instrument for emissions reduction.'

3. Напишите одно и то же наблюдение о климате в трёх регистрах: informal, neutral, formal.

💭 Рефлексия: Какой регистр труднее всего контролировать — и почему? Что происходит, когда регистр «соскальзывает» в середине предложения?

📙 Grammar 2 · Lexical Register Upgrade: замена слов по регистровой лестнице

Verbs · Adjectives · Connectors · Reporting verbs — систематическая Latinate замена
🟣

Знать академические слова — недостаточно.

Нужно знать почему одно слово академичнее другого при одном значении — и уметь выбирать точно, а не случайно.
Категория Informal/Neutral Formal upgrade Ещё более formal Разница в оттенке
Глагол изменения go up / rise increase / escalate undergo sustained escalation / record a pronounced upward trajectory 'Undergo' — пассивная претерпевость; 'record' — фиксация данных
Глагол показа show demonstrate / reveal indicate / attest to / bear witness to 'Attest to' — формально-юридический; 'bear witness' — риторически сильный
Глагол утверждения say / think argue / contend submit / posit / aver 'Submit' — юридический; 'posit' — философский
Глагол решения fix / deal with address / resolve redress / ameliorate / mitigate 'Redress' — исправление неравенства; 'ameliorate' — облегчение; 'mitigate' — снижение риска
Прилагательное важности big / important significant / major consequential / material / non-trivial 'Material' — юридический термин; 'consequential' — с долгосрочными последствиями
Прилагательное плохого bad / terrible problematic / harmful deleterious / pernicious / inimical to 'Pernicious' — незаметно разрушительный; 'inimical to' — враждебный чему-либо
Коннектор уступки but however / nevertheless notwithstanding / be that as it may 'Notwithstanding' — формальное признание; 'be that as it may' — риторическое (C11)
Коннектор причины because / so therefore / consequently it follows that / by extension 'By extension' — логический вывод (C11); 'it follows that' — дедукция

⚡ Reporting verbs: регистровая иерархия

Level 1 (neutral): says, thinks, believes, shows

Level 2 (academic): argues, contends, suggests, demonstrates, reveals, indicates

Level 3 (high formal): posits, submits, avers, asserts, advances the claim that, takes the position that

Тонкость: reporting verb тоже выражает отношение автора к источнику. 'Suggests' — осторожно. 'Demonstrates' — принимает как доказанное. 'Claims' — дистанцируется или сомневается. 'Asserts' — подчёркивает, что это мнение, не факт.
Задача Weak (Band 5–6) Strong (Band 7–8) Регистровый механизм
Описать тренд Emissions have gone up a lot in recent years. Global greenhouse gas emissions have undergone a sustained and pronounced upward trajectory over the past three decades, notwithstanding periodic fluctuations attributable to economic contractions. undergo + NP + notwithstanding + reduced participial
Описать провал политики The government didn't do what it needed to do. Successive administrations have demonstrably failed to translate the scientific consensus on climate risk into commensurate regulatory action. demonstrably (sentence adverb) + Latinate verb + nominalised NP
Выразить необходимость We really need to act now. The imperative for immediate, substantive, and internationally coordinated policy reform is, I would contend, now incontrovertible. nominalised 'imperative' + stacked pre-modifiers + epistemic parenthetical
Процитировать позицию Some scientists say this is dangerous. A growing body of empirical literature contends that the current trajectory of greenhouse gas accumulation poses existential risks to the biophysical systems upon which human civilisation depends. reporting verb 'contends' + complex NP subject + relative clause with formal 'upon which'
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Renewable energy is getting more and more popular.

Правильно: Renewable energy technologies have achieved substantial and accelerating market penetration, driven by declining production costs and increasingly supportive regulatory environments.

Почему: 'Getting more and more popular' — разговорное прогрессивное. В формальном тексте: nominalised NP + passive с причинной клаузой

Ошибка: Claims has been made that climate change is fake.

Правильно: It has been asserted by a small number of commentators — contrary to the overwhelming scientific consensus — that anthropogenic climate change is overstated.

Почему: 'Claims has been made' — ошибочное согласование. Правильно: 'Claims have been made' или лучше: 'It has been asserted that' (impersonal + formal register)
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Lexical Register

✔ Latinate verb ('ameliorate', 'mitigate', 'attenuate') вместо phrasal → немедленный LR Band 7+ сигнал

✔ Reporting verb с точным эпистемическим значением ('contends' vs 'claims' vs 'demonstrates') → TA + LR

✔ Nominalisation вместо phrasal verb ('undergo a reduction' vs 'go down') → формальность + плотность (GRA + LR)
⚠ Снижает балл: Latinate слово ради латинского — пустой формализм; экзаменатор ценит точность, а не длину слова
📝 Задание 2 · Lexical Register Upgrade

1. Поднимите на два уровня регистра: (a) 'The rise in sea levels is a big problem for coastal cities.' (b) 'Scientists think that we need to cut carbon emissions quickly.' (c) 'Countries are starting to work together on climate, but it's not enough.' (d) 'The bad effects of climate change are getting worse.'

2. Для каждого предложения укажите: (i) какое слово/структуру вы заменили, (ii) какой механизм использовали (nominalisation / Latinate verb / impersonal / reporting verb), (iii) что изменилось в оттенке значения.

3. Напишите абзац (60–70 слов) о климатической политике, используя минимум 4 Latinate verb и 2 nominalisation.

💭 Рефлексия: Всегда ли более формальное слово лучше? Когда точный нейтральный термин предпочтительнее латинского эквивалента?

📗 Grammar 3 · Syntactic Register: формальные синтаксические структуры

Preposition fronting · Formal negation · Cleft emphasis · Impersonal it · Non-finite clauses
🟣

Регистр — не только словарь.

В уровне C мы изучаем синтаксические структуры, которые сами по себе сигнализируют о формальном регистре — независимо от выбранных слов.
Структура Informal/Neutral Formal equivalent Почему формальнее
Preposition fronting (relative clause) the policy that we rely on the policy upon which we rely / the framework within which decisions are made Предлог перед relative pronoun = письменный академический стандарт
Formal negation not... any no / none of 'No significant evidence' звучит формальнее, чем 'not any significant evidence'
Fronted negative (C9) We have never seen this before. Never before have such challenges confronted policymakers. Инверсия после фронтированного отрицания = высокий формальный регистр
Formal quantifiers a lot of / lots of a considerable number of / a substantial proportion of / the overwhelming majority of Точные количественные NP вместо разговорных
Cleft for emphasis The main problem is money. It is the inadequacy of financial commitment, rather than technical capacity, that constitutes the primary obstacle. It-cleft с 'rather than' = формальная эмфаза + разграничение (C9)
Non-finite clause instead of 'because' Because emissions rose, temperatures increased. Emissions having risen sharply, a corresponding increase in global mean temperatures became statistically inevitable. Nominative absolute: высший формальный уровень
Impersonal it + formal verb People know that... / It's clear that... It is widely acknowledged / It has been demonstrated / It bears noting that Impersonal it = максимальная прагматическая дистанция (C13)

📐 Nominative Absolute: самая формальная non-finite clause

Обычная причинная клауза: 'Because emissions have risen sharply, temperatures have increased.'

Nominative absolute: 'Emissions having risen sharply, a corresponding increase in global temperatures has become statistically inevitable.'

Структура: NP + having + past participle / being + past participle , main clause.

Другие примеры:

'All regulatory options having been exhausted, the case for binding international treaty obligations becomes overwhelming.'

'The scientific consensus being now beyond reasonable dispute, the remaining question is one of political will rather than epistemic uncertainty.'

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

Never before in the recorded history of international climate diplomacy have the technical means for a managed energy transition been more readily available, nor the political will to deploy them more conspicuously absent — a paradox that is, upon which one might reflect, attributable less to ignorance than to the structural incentives governing the behaviour of states and corporations in a competitive international order.

① Never before... have — fronted negative inversion (C9): highest formal register; rhetorical force

② nor the political will... more conspicuously absent — parallel negative structure with ellipsis: Band 8 CC

③ upon which one might reflect — fronted preposition relative + formal impersonal 'one': double formality signal

④ attributable less to ignorance than to the structural incentives — Latinate adjective + 'less... than' formal comparison
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: The policy that we depend on is not working.

Правильно: The policy framework upon which effective climate governance depends has demonstrably failed to produce the emissions reductions it was designed to achieve.

Почему: 'The policy that we depend on' — informal relative + personal pronoun. Формальный вариант: 'upon which... depends' + impersonal subject

Ошибка: There are a lot of reasons why countries don't cooperate.

Правильно: A considerable number of structural and political factors militate against the kind of sustained international cooperation that meaningful climate governance requires.

Почему: 'A lot of reasons why' — разговорное. Замени: 'a considerable number of factors' + Latinate verb 'militate against' + formal relative
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Syntactic Register

✔ 'Upon which / within which / through which' → мгновенный LR + GRA сигнал; редко используется студентами Band 6

✔ Fronted negative inversion ('Never before have...') → самая эффектная формальная структура для кульминационного аргумента

✔ Nominative absolute ('The evidence having been reviewed...') → Band 8-exclusive structure; немедленно замечается экзаменатором
⚠ Снижает балл: Nominative absolute без правильного синтаксиса ('Emissions having rose...') — грубая ошибка; используй только если уверен в форме
📝 Задание 3 · Syntactic Register

1. Трансформируйте в формальный синтаксис: (a) 'The targets that countries agreed to are not being met.' → используй 'upon which' или 'to which'. (b) 'Because many countries failed to act, the problem got worse.' → nominative absolute. (c) 'The main issue is that rich countries don't pay enough.' → it-cleft с 'rather than'.

2. Добавьте fronted negative inversion для максимальной риторической силы: (d) 'We have never faced a more urgent climate challenge.' (e) 'The scientific evidence has rarely been clearer.'

3. Напишите 2 предложения о климате, используя: (f) nominative absolute + main clause, (g) never before + inversion + nor-parallel.

💭 Рефлексия: Когда fronted negative inversion уместна — а когда она звучит искусственно? Как понять, что ты не перегнул с формальностью?

📕 Grammar 4 · Register Consistency & Band 8 Upgrade

Полный регистровый контроль: как избежать сдвигов и выдержать формальность от первого слова до последнего
🟣

Самая частая регистровая проблема Band 6–7 — не незнание формальных структур.

Это непоследовательность: два формальных предложения — одно разговорное — снова формальное. Экзаменатор замечает каждый сдвиг. В Grammar 4 мы строим систему регистрового контроля.
Источник регистрового сдвига Типичный пример Исправление Правило
Phrasal verb в формальном контексте The policy was set up to reduce emissions. The policy was designed to achieve measurable reductions in greenhouse gas emissions. Заменяй все phrasal verbs на Latinate одночленные глаголы
Personal pronoun в академическом эссе I believe that renewable energy is the best solution. It is submitted that renewable energy technologies represent the most technically viable pathway to decarbonisation. Замени 'I believe/think/feel' на impersonal или hedged formal
Contraction It's widely known that emissions are rising. It is widely acknowledged that greenhouse gas concentrations are undergoing a sustained upward trajectory. Никаких contractions в IELTS Writing — без исключений
Vague quantifier A lot of countries are trying to reduce emissions. An increasing number of national governments have undertaken formal commitments to emissions reduction targets. 'A lot of' → 'a considerable number of / a substantial proportion of / the overwhelming majority of'
Emotive intensifier Climate change is a really serious problem. Anthropogenic climate change constitutes one of the most consequential governance challenges of the contemporary era. 'Really / very / extremely' → degree adverb + formal evaluation: 'demonstrably / arguably / profoundly'
Informal connector Also, renewable energy creates jobs. Moreover, the expansion of the renewable energy sector has demonstrably generated significant employment in regions previously dependent upon fossil fuel extraction. 'Also/And/But' at sentence start → formal connector + full formal clause

📐 Register Audit Checklist — проверяй перед каждой сдачей

□ Нет contractions (it's, don't, can't)?

□ Нет phrasal verbs там, где есть Latinate замена?

□ Нет 'I think / I believe / in my opinion'?

□ Нет 'a lot of / lots of / loads of'?

□ Нет 'really / very / extremely' перед прилагательным (замени на 'profoundly / demonstrably / arguably')?

□ Нет 'And / But / Also' в начале предложения (замени на формальный коннектор)?

□ Все relative clauses с 'that' проверены: не лучше ли 'upon which / within which / through which'?

□ Все причинные клаузы 'because' проверены: не лучше ли nominative absolute или given that / in light of?
🚀 Band 8 Upgrade

Band 6: Climate change is a really big problem and countries need to work together to fix it. I think renewable energy is the best solution.

Band 7: Climate change represents a significant global challenge that requires coordinated international action. Renewable energy technologies arguably offer the most promising pathway to sustainable decarbonisation.

Band 8: Anthropogenic climate change constitutes, notwithstanding the scientific consensus having been established beyond reasonable dispute for over three decades, one of the most consequential governance failures of the contemporary era — a failure attributable not to epistemic uncertainty but to the structural misalignment between the temporal horizons of democratic accountability and the geological timescales upon which climate systems operate.
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Register Consistency

✔ Абсолютная последовательность формального регистра на 250 словах → LR Band 8: это редко и немедленно замечается

✔ Register Audit Checklist перед сдачей → самый надёжный способ повысить LR на полбалла

✔ Формальный регистр + точность + разнообразие → три компонента LR Band 8 одновременно
⚠ Снижает балл: Один разговорный элемент в 250-словном эссе ('it's', 'a lot of', 'I think') снижает LR до Band 6 — непропорционально сильный штраф
📝 Задание 4 · Register Consistency

1. Проведите Register Audit следующего абзаца и исправьте все нарушения: 'Climate change is getting worse and worse, and it's really scary. A lot of countries are trying to do something about it, but they're not doing enough. I think the main problem is that governments are too worried about the economy. Also, renewable energy is getting cheaper, which is good. But we really need to hurry up.'

2. Подсчитайте количество регистровых нарушений в исходном абзаце. Для каждого: (a) тип нарушения, (b) исправление, (c) какая ось регистра затронута (лексика / синтаксис / прагматика).

3. Напишите абзац (80–90 слов) о международном климатическом сотрудничестве в безупречно формальном регистре. Затем пройдите свой собственный Register Audit Checklist.

💭 Рефлексия: Возьмите свой последний IELTS эссе. Проведите Register Audit. Сколько нарушений? Какой тип встречается чаще всего?

📖 Reading 1 · The Political Economy of Climate Inaction

Академическое чтение: безупречный формальный регистр в реальном тексте
📖 The Political Economy of Climate Inaction: Structural Incentives, Temporal Horizons, and the Limits of International Cooperation

It is widely acknowledged, within the scholarly literature on environmental governance, that the failure of successive international climate agreements to produce emissions reductions commensurate with the scale of the physical challenge is attributable not primarily to a deficit of technical knowledge or financial capacity but to the structural misalignment between the temporal horizons governing political decision-making and the geological timescales upon which the consequences of current emissions will be most acutely felt. Crucially, the costs of ambitious climate action are concentrated in the present electoral cycle, while the most significant benefits accrue to populations not yet born — a temporal asymmetry that no existing international institutional architecture has yet proved capable of resolving.

Notwithstanding the formal commitments undertaken by the majority of the world's major economies under successive international agreements, the gap between stated policy objectives and actual emissions trajectories has, it must be acknowledged, widened rather than narrowed over the three decades since the scientific consensus on anthropogenic climate change was established beyond reasonable dispute. More precisely, it is not the absence of international agreements but the demonstrably inadequate mechanisms for monitoring, enforcement, and accountability within those agreements that constitutes the binding constraint upon effective collective action.

What the foregoing analysis suggests, I would contend, is that the prevailing approach to international climate governance — premised as it is upon the voluntary commitments of sovereign states operating within a system of formally equal but substantively asymmetric bargaining power — is structurally incapable of producing outcomes proportionate to the scale of the challenge. Never before have the technical preconditions for a managed global energy transition been more thoroughly established; yet the political preconditions for translating that technical capacity into binding collective action remain, it would appear, as elusive as ever.

📝 Задания к Reading 1 — Регистровый анализ

1. Найдите в тексте минимум 5 Latinate глаголов. Для каждого: (a) запишите более простой нейтральный эквивалент, (b) объясните, что теряется при замене (оттенок значения, регистр, точность).

2. 'Upon which the consequences of current emissions will be most acutely felt' — preposition fronting. Перепишите в informal/neutral варианте с 'that'. Затем объясните: почему 'upon which' здесь точнее, чем просто 'which'?

3. Финальный абзац начинается с 'Never before have...' — fronted negative inversion. Что именно создаёт этот приём в контексте всего абзаца? Перепишите без инверсии. Что теряется?

4. Проведите Register Audit текста: найдите ли вы хотя бы один элемент, который кажется менее формальным, чем остальной текст? Если нет — почему последовательность важна?

5. Текст использует три разных механизма для выражения неудачи климатической политики без прямого обвинения: (a) nominalisation, (b) agentless passive (C10), (c) impersonal it-clause (C13). Найдите по одному примеру каждого и объясните, как каждый избегает прямого указания на виновника.

📚 Словарь урока — C1–C2 Academic Vocabulary (30 слов)

Слово Значение / Коллокация Пример
decarbonisation декарбонизация; pathway to decarbonisation / accelerate decarbonisation The pathway to decarbonisation requires both technological innovation and fundamental shifts in energy governance.
mitigation смягчение; climate mitigation / mitigation strategy / mitigation potential Mitigation strategies aim to reduce the magnitude of future climate change by limiting emissions.
adaptation адаптация; climate adaptation / adaptation capacity / adapt to change Adaptation measures address the consequences of climate change that can no longer be prevented.
commensurate соразмерный, адекватный; commensurate with / commensurate response The policy response has not been commensurate with the scale of the scientific evidence.
trajectory траектория; emissions trajectory / current trajectory / deviate from Current emissions trajectories are inconsistent with the targets established under the Paris Agreement.
sovereignty суверенитет; national sovereignty / exercise of sovereignty Climate governance is complicated by the tension between national sovereignty and collective action imperatives.
asymmetric асимметричный; asymmetric bargaining / asymmetric burden The burdens and benefits of climate action are distributed asymmetrically across nations and generations.
incumbent обязательный; incumbent upon / it is incumbent on governments to It is incumbent upon high-income countries to finance the decarbonisation of developing economies.
incontrovertible неопровержимый; incontrovertible evidence / incontrovertible consensus The scientific case for urgent climate action is now incontrovertible.
militate препятствовать; militate against / structural factors that militate Several structural factors militate against the kind of sustained cooperation that climate governance requires.
remediation устранение, исправление; environmental remediation / remediation strategy Carbon capture technologies are proposed as a remediation strategy for atmospheric CO2 concentrations.
renewable возобновляемый; renewable energy / renewable sources / transition to renewables The costs of renewable energy have fallen dramatically over the past decade.
anthropogenic антропогенный; anthropogenic climate change / anthropogenic emissions The anthropogenic origin of current climate change is not scientifically disputed.
regime режим; international regime / regulatory regime / climate regime The international climate regime is based on voluntary commitments rather than binding enforcement.
ratification ратификация; ratification of a treaty / ratify commitments The effectiveness of international agreements depends upon universal ratification and implementation.
externality внешний эффект; negative externality / carbon externality / internalise Carbon pricing aims to internalise the negative externalities of fossil fuel combustion.
geopolitical геополитический; geopolitical rivalry / geopolitical constraints Geopolitical rivalry between major powers has complicated multilateral climate negotiations.
net zero углеродная нейтральность; net zero target / pathway to net zero Many governments have committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2050.
incumbent (adj) нынешний, занимающий должность; incumbent government / incumbent administration Incumbent governments face electoral pressures that often override long-term climate commitments.
stranded asset обесценившийся актив; stranded asset risk / fossil fuel stranded assets A rapid transition to renewables could strand trillions of dollars in fossil fuel assets.
proliferation распространение; proliferation of renewables / rapid proliferation The proliferation of solar technology has been driven primarily by dramatic cost reductions.
consensus консенсус; scientific consensus / build consensus / consensus position The scientific consensus on anthropogenic climate change is not genuinely contested within the research community.
accountability подотчётность; accountability mechanism / lack of accountability International climate agreements lack robust accountability mechanisms for non-compliance.
leverage влияние, рычаг; leverage policy / leverage change / financial leverage Carbon pricing can leverage private investment into low-carbon technologies at scale.
endogenous эндогенный; endogenous growth / endogenous factor Technological innovation in clean energy is increasingly understood as an endogenous policy outcome.
exacerbate усугублять; exacerbate inequality / exacerbate the problem Poorly designed climate policies risk exacerbating existing socioeconomic inequalities.
stipulate предусматривать, обязывать; stipulate requirements / as stipulated The agreement stipulates that parties submit updated nationally determined contributions every five years.
temporal временной; temporal horizon / temporal asymmetry The temporal asymmetry between political cycles and climate timescales undermines effective governance.
binding обязательный; legally binding / binding commitment / binding framework A legally binding international carbon price remains the most robust proposed instrument.
compel вынуждать, обязывать; compel action / legally compelled No international body currently has the authority to compel states to meet their climate commitments.

📖 Reading 2 · The Case for a Global Carbon Price

Формальный регистр в политическом аргументе
📖 The Case for a Global Carbon Price: Economic Efficiency, Political Feasibility, and Distributional Justice

Among the instruments available to the international community for addressing the challenge of climate change, a globally coordinated carbon pricing mechanism is, arguably, the most economically efficient. The case for such a mechanism rests upon the straightforward observation that greenhouse gas emissions constitute a negative externality whose costs are borne not by those who generate them but by the global commons — a situation that, in the absence of corrective pricing, will persist indefinitely and whose consequences, it must be acknowledged, will be felt most acutely by those who have contributed least to its genesis.

Notwithstanding the theoretical elegance of this argument, the political obstacles to the implementation of a binding global carbon price are, it would appear, formidable. The asymmetric distribution of abatement costs across national economies — with carbon-intensive emerging economies facing a disproportionate burden relative to their historical contribution to atmospheric concentrations — constitutes, in the judgement of many analysts, the most significant structural impediment to the construction of a durable international consensus. Never before have the competing imperatives of economic development, historical equity, and physical necessity been more sharply in tension.

What the evidence therefore suggests is that the question of global carbon pricing is, at its core, not a technical but a political one: not a matter of identifying the optimal price signal but of constructing the institutional architecture within which sovereign states can be induced — rather than compelled — to accept constraints upon their emissions that are perceived as equitable, enforceable, and compatible with continued economic development. It is submitted, I would contend, that achieving this requires not merely more sophisticated economic modelling but a fundamental rethinking of the governance frameworks through which international environmental cooperation is structured and legitimated.

📝 Задания к Reading 2 — Регистр и аргументация

1. 'A globally coordinated carbon pricing mechanism is, arguably, the most economically efficient.' Разберите все регистровые маркеры в этом предложении: лексика (Latinate?), синтаксис (структура NP?), прагматика (sentence adverb?).

2. 'Never before have the competing imperatives... been more sharply in tension.' Это fronted negative inversion. Почему автор выбрал именно это место для инверсии? Что должно было бы стоять здесь без инверсии — и как это меняет риторический эффект?

3. 'through which international environmental cooperation is structured and legitimated' — preposition fronting + formal passive. Перепишите в informal варианте. Подсчитайте количество регистровых изменений, которые вы сделали.

4. Текст содержит 'It is submitted, I would contend' — это два epistemic markers подряд. Один ('It is submitted') — impersonal, другой ('I would contend') — personal. Зачем автор использует оба? Это избыточность или намеренный риторический приём?

5. Напишите продолжение (80–90 слов) в том же формальном регистре, используя: Never before + inversion → notwithstanding → it is submitted that → upon which. Проведите Register Audit своего абзаца.

✍️ Writing Task 1 · Line Graph Description

Формальный регистр в описании данных: никаких phrasal verbs и vague language
✍️ Writing Task 1 · Line Graph (170 слов, цель: Band 7.5)

Задание:

The graph below shows the share of electricity generated from renewable sources in five countries (Germany, China, USA, India, and Brazil) between 2000 and 2022. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

📌 Model Opening — Band 8:

The data reveal a pattern of broadly divergent trajectories in renewable electricity generation across the five countries surveyed, with the most pronounced upward trend recorded in Germany — a development attributable, in no small measure, to the sustained policy commitment embodied in successive iterations of the Energiewende framework — and the most substantial absolute increase in installed renewable capacity achieved by China, notwithstanding its considerably lower proportional share of total generation.

① broadly divergent trajectories — formal NP: nominalised 'trajectories' + Latinate 'divergent' + hedging 'broadly'

② the most pronounced upward trend — formal superlative NP: Latinate 'pronounced' instead of 'big/large'

③ a development attributable, in no small measure, to — appositive NP + formal expression ('in no small measure' = formal understatement)

④ notwithstanding its considerably lower proportional share — formal concession marker (C11) in mid-sentence

👁 What the Examiner Sees:

TA (7–8): Главный паттерн (divergence) + две выделенные страны с разными обоснованиями

CC (7–8): Appositive NP + notwithstanding: два уровня структуры в одном предложении

LR (7–8): trajectories, pronounced, attributable, embodied, proportional — все Latinate, нет phrasal verbs

GRA (7–8): Complex NP subject + appositive + concession в одном предложении

📐 Структура эссе:

Overview (2 пр.): Formal NP для главного паттерна. Notwithstanding для ключевого исключения.

Body 1 (3–4 пр.): Latinate verbs для трендов: 'undergo a sustained increase / record significant growth / achieve'. Never before для кульминации.

Body 2 (3–4 пр.): Preposition fronting для сравнений: 'relative to which / in comparison with which'. Nominative absolute для причин.

✍️ Writing Task 2 · Discursive Essay

Безупречный формальный регистр на 270 словах: от первого слова до последнего
✍️ Writing Task 2 · Discursive Essay (270 слов, цель: Band 7.5)

Задание:

Some argue that individual lifestyle changes — such as reducing meat consumption, using public transport, and minimising air travel — are the most effective way to address climate change. Others believe that systemic changes, such as government regulation and corporate responsibility, are more important. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

📌 Model Opening — Band 8:

It is widely contended that the discourse surrounding individual versus systemic responsibility for climate change reflects, in microcosm, a more fundamental tension within environmental governance between the liberal emphasis upon individual agency and the structural argument that the causes of — and solutions to — anthropogenic climate change are irreducibly collective in nature.

① It is widely contended that — impersonal passive (C13) + formal reporting: maximum pragmatic distance

② in microcosm — formal Latin phrase as adverbial: signals high register immediately

③ the liberal emphasis upon individual agency — preposition fronting ('upon') + formal NP

④ irreducibly collective in nature — Latinate adverb + adjective: Band 8 evaluative phrase

👁 What the Examiner Sees:

TA (7–8): Оба взгляда представлены без caricature; собственная позиция выражена через formal hedged structures

CC (7–8): It is widely contended → Notwithstanding → Never before have → It is submitted: четыре разных формальных маркера

LR (7–8): Полный отказ от phrasal verbs, contractions, vague language — Register Audit пройден

GRA (7–8): Impersonal + preposition fronting + nominative absolute + fronted negative в разных частях эссе

📐 Структура эссе:

Intro (2 пр.): It is widely contended + in microcosm. Тезис с 'I would contend' в конце.

View 1 — Individual (3–4 пр.): Latinate verbs + preposition fronting. 'Notwithstanding its intuitive appeal...'

View 2 — Systemic (3–4 пр.): Nominative absolute + formal comparison. 'Never before have structural incentives been more clearly...'

Own Opinion + Conclusion (3 пр.): It is submitted that... / attributable not to X but to Y / upon which effective governance depends.

🎓 Урок C15 завершён!

✅ Register Continuum: три оси (лексика / синтаксис / прагматика) и пять уровней от informal до hyper-formal

✅ Lexical Register Upgrade: Latinate verbs / reporting verbs / formal quantifiers / precise evaluative adjectives

✅ Syntactic Register: preposition fronting / nominative absolute / fronted negative inversion / impersonal it-clause

✅ Register Consistency: Register Audit Checklist — восемь типов нарушений и их исправление

✅ Band 8 Register = последовательный формальный регистр + точность + разнообразие на протяжении всего эссе

💡 Central Question — Ответ:

Один и тот же смысл в разных регистрах — это разные сообщения, потому что регистр кодирует не только содержание, но и отношение: между автором и читателем, между автором и предметом, между высказыванием и истиной. 'I think this is bad' — это мнение человека. 'It is widely acknowledged that this constitutes a consequential failure' — это позиция, встроенная в институциональный дискурс. Смысл похож. Регистр — разный. И именно это различие IELTS измеряет как Lexical Resource.

▶ Следующий урок — C16: Ellipsis & Substitution. Ellipsis & Substitution — so / not / do so / the former / such. Как избегать повторения, не теряя точности?
C16:
A to C

IELTS FROM ZERO

Урок C16 · Upper-Intermediate / Advanced

Ellipsis & Substitution

Как избегать повторения, не теряя точности?

Тематика: Глобализация и экономическое неравенство · Словарь: C1–C2 Academic · Writing: Cause & Effect Essay

💥 Урочный крючок — Зачем это нужно?

Прочитай эти два абзаца об экономическом неравенстве:

Band 6 (с повторением): Globalisation has increased inequality. Globalisation has benefited some countries but not other countries. Some countries have developed quickly. Other countries have not developed quickly. Developed countries support globalisation. Developing countries do not always support globalisation.

Band 8 (с ellipsis & substitution): Globalisation has increased inequality, benefiting some economies considerably while leaving others largely behind. Developed nations have tended to support the liberalisation agenda; developing ones have not — or have done so only selectively, and with good reason.

Band 6 повторяет каждое слово, потому что боится быть непонятым. Band 8 доверяет читателю — и использует пропуски и замены, которые делают текст не короче, а плотнее, быстрее, и умнее. Именно это и оценивает IELTS как Cohesion & Coherence.

📘 Grammar 1 · Verbal Ellipsis: пропуск глагольной группы

Auxiliary ellipsis · do/does/did · So/Neither · Comparative ellipsis
🟣

В уровне A ты знал: 'Can you speak French? — Yes, I can.' (пропуск глагола после auxiliary)

В уровне B ты расширил: 'She works harder than he does.'

В уровне C мы изучаем глагольную эллипсис в академическом тексте — как она создаёт плотность и параллелизм без потери точности.
Тип эллипсиса Структура Пример без эллипсиса С эллипсисом Функция
Auxiliary ellipsis S + aux (+ not) Some nations have adopted carbon pricing; other nations have not adopted carbon pricing. Some nations have adopted carbon pricing; others have not. Избегает повторения VP; 'have not' несёт всю нагрузку
Do-substitution do / does / did Wealthy countries consume more resources than poor countries consume. Wealthy countries consume more resources than poor ones do. 'do' замещает весь предикат
So / Neither inversion So + aux + S / Neither + aux + S Globalisation has increased productivity. Globalisation has also reduced labour costs. Globalisation has increased productivity — and so, in many contexts, has it reduced labour costs. 'So' = подтверждение того же предиката; инверсия сигнализирует о formal register
Comparative ellipsis more/less/as + adj + as The economic benefits of trade liberalisation are less evenly distributed than the economic costs of trade liberalisation. The economic benefits of trade liberalisation are less evenly distributed than the costs. Полный VP пропускается после точки сравнения
Parallel clause ellipsis Shared predicate omitted Some regions have benefited from globalisation; some regions have not benefited from globalisation. Some regions have benefited from globalisation; others have not — and the divergence is widening. Эллипсис + добавление нового элемента в одной структуре

⚡ So/Neither inversion в академическом тексте

Стандартное: 'Trade liberalisation has increased output. It has also widened inequality.'

С 'so' + инверсией: 'Trade liberalisation has increased aggregate output — and so, it would appear, has it systematically widened the distribution of that output's benefits.'

Ключевое правило: 'so' стоит после тире или точки с запятой, затем — инверсия aux + subject. Sentence adverbs из C13 ('it would appear') можно встраивать между 'so' и инверсией.

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

High-income economies have largely succeeded in exporting the environmental costs of manufacturing to lower-income ones — and so, it would appear, have they exported a commensurate share of the employment and technological development that might otherwise have accrued to their own populations.

① have largely succeeded in exporting — verbal + gerund: 'exporting' carries the semantic weight

② lower-income ones — nominal substitution: 'ones' replaces 'economies' (Grammar 2 preview)

③ and so, it would appear, have they — so-inversion + parenthetical hedging (C13): formal, high-density

④ a commensurate share of the employment and technological development that — complex NP object with defining relative: only ellipted what can be recovered
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Some countries have benefited and others countries have not benefited.
