A to C

IELTS FROM ZERO

Урок C17 · Upper-Intermediate / Advanced

Parallel Structures & Rhetorical Balance

Как симметрия делает аргумент убедительнее?

Тематика: Образование и социальная мобильность · Словарь: C1–C2 Academic · Writing: Problem-Solution Essay

💥 Урочный крючок — Зачем это нужно?

Два финала эссе об образовании и мобильности:

Band 6: Education is important for social mobility. Without education, people cannot get good jobs. Governments should invest more in education because it will help society.

Band 8: Education systems that expand access not merely to credentials but to genuine intellectual formation — not merely to qualifications but to the capacity for critical thought — are not simply an instrument of economic development; they are, above all, a mechanism through which societies reproduce themselves as genuinely open rather than merely nominally meritocratic.

Посмотри на Band 8: 'not merely to X but to Y — not merely to X but to Y'. Это не просто повторение. Это риторический баланс — структура, которая несёт смысл через симметрию. Читатель чувствует, что два элемента сопоставлены, взвешены, оценены. Именно это и есть риторическая сила параллельных структур.

📘 Grammar 1 · Correlative Conjunctions: парные союзы как риторические весы

Not only X but also Y · Not X but Y · Both X and Y · Neither X nor Y · Either X or Y · Not so much X as Y
🟣

В уровне A ты знал: 'not only... but also', 'both... and', 'neither... nor'.

В уровне C мы изучаем их риторическую функцию: каждый парный союз создаёт разные весы — разные отношения между двумя элементами.
Конструкция Риторическая функция Формальные требования Академический пример
Not only X but (also) Y Добавление с усилением: Y важнее или неожиданнее X Строгий параллелизм: X и Y одного синтаксического типа Educational inequality perpetuates not only economic disadvantage but also, and more insidiously, the systematic attenuation of intellectual aspiration itself.
Not X but Y Замена / коррекция: X — ложное, Y — истинное X и Y — прямая оппозиция; Y опровергает X The primary obstacle to social mobility is not the absence of talent in disadvantaged communities but the structural suppression of the conditions under which talent can develop and be recognised.
Not merely X but Y Уточнение масштаба: Y больше, чем предполагает X Y включает X и превосходит его Education must address not merely the transmission of knowledge but the cultivation of the reflective capacity that enables individuals to interrogate and extend that knowledge.
Not so much X as Y Переключение акцента: X менее важен, чем Y X уменьшается, Y усиливается; тонкое разграничение The challenge is not so much the lack of financial investment as the structural disincentives that prevent existing resources from reaching those who most need them.
Both X and Y Двойное включение: оба истинны X и Y — равнозначные элементы одной категории Both the content of the curriculum and the pedagogical methods through which it is delivered require fundamental reform.
Neither X nor Y Двойное исключение: ни один не истинен Отрицание обоих элементов как неправильных ответов Neither increased spending alone nor structural reform alone is sufficient; what is required is their coordinated and sustained combination.

⚡ Not only X but also Y: правило инверсии при фронтинге

Стандартная позиция (X в середине предложения): 'Education perpetuates not only economic disadvantage but also cognitive deprivation.'

Fronted (X в начале — C9 + инверсия): 'Not only does education perpetuate economic disadvantage; it also systematically suppresses the cognitive ambitions of those born into poverty.'

Правило: Когда 'Not only' стоит в начале предложения, главная клауза требует инверсии (does/is/has + subject). Это комбинация Fronting (C9) + Correlative conjunction (C17).

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

The persistent underrepresentation of students from lower socioeconomic backgrounds in elite educational institutions reflects not merely the unequal distribution of financial resources but, more fundamentally, the systematic reproduction of cultural capital — the tacit knowledge, the linguistic confidence, the horizon of legitimate aspiration — that elite environments both require and produce.

① not merely X but, more fundamentally, Y — 'not merely... but' + sentence adverb внутри: Y значительно важнее X

② the tacit knowledge, the linguistic confidence, the horizon of legitimate aspiration — tricolon: три параллельных NP, каждый с определителем

③ that elite environments both require and produce — 'both X and Y': двойное включение в одном relative clause

④ cultural capital — Bourdieu's term: precise sociological register signals TA
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Education is not only important for jobs but also it helps society.

Правильно: Education is important not only for individual economic outcomes but also for the reproduction of the social trust upon which democratic governance depends.

Почему: Параллелизм нарушен: 'for jobs' (PP) vs 'it helps society' (clause). 'Not only X but also Y' требует одинаковой синтаксической формы для X и Y

Ошибка: Neither the government or the private sector can solve this alone.

Правильно: Neither the government nor the private sector, acting in isolation, possesses the capacity to address the structural roots of educational inequality.

Почему: 'Neither... or' — неправильно. Только: 'neither... nor'. 'Neither... or' — распространённая ошибка, немедленно замечается экзаменатором
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Correlative Conjunctions

✔ 'Not X but Y' для коррекции ложной дихотомии → Band 8 TA: автор точно формулирует реальную проблему

✔ 'Not merely X but Y' → показывает концептуальную глубину: X верно, но недостаточно

✔ Fronted 'Not only' + inversion → комбинация C9 + C17 = редкая, безупречно формальная структура
⚠ Снижает балл: 'Not only... but also' с нарушенным параллелизмом (разные синтаксические формы) — систематическая ошибка Band 6; снижает GRA
📝 Задание 1 · Correlative Conjunctions

1. Перепишите, используя указанный correlative conjunction и строгий параллелизм: (a) 'Education helps the economy. Education also helps society.' → not only... but also. (b) 'The problem is not money. The real problem is structural inequality.' → not X but Y. (c) 'Expanding access is important. Improving quality is also important.' → both X and Y. (d) 'Throwing money at the problem won't work. Passing new laws alone won't work either.' → neither X nor Y.

2. Добавьте fronted 'Not only' с инверсией: (e) 'Educational systems not only fail to promote mobility; they actively reproduce existing hierarchies.' → 'Not only do educational systems...'

3. Напишите 3 предложения об образовании и мобильности, используя 'not merely X but Y', 'not so much X as Y', и 'neither X nor Y'. Объясните риторическую функцию каждого.

💭 Рефлексия: В чём разница между 'not X but Y' и 'not so much X as Y'? Когда точнее первое, а когда второе?

📙 Grammar 2 · Comparative Parallelism: симметрия через сравнение

The more X, the more Y · The greater X, the more Y · As X increases, so Y · X, just as Y
🟣

Сравнительные параллельные структуры — это не просто грамматика.

В уровне C они становятся аналитическим инструментом: автор не просто утверждает корреляцию — он структурно демонстрирует её через форму самого предложения.
Конструкция Значение Синтаксические требования Пример
The more X, the more/less Y Пропорциональная зависимость: рост X → рост/падение Y Оба клауза начинаются с 'the + comparative'; подлежащее может быть в любой позиции The more stratified an education system becomes, the less it functions as an instrument of social mobility and the more it operates as a mechanism for the intergenerational reproduction of advantage.
The greater/higher/deeper X, the more/greater Y Та же логика с существительными вместо прилагательных 'The + comparative adj + NP' вместо 'The more + adj' The greater the degree of socioeconomic segregation in schooling, the more pronounced the divergence in cognitive and non-cognitive outcomes between advantaged and disadvantaged pupils.
As X increases/grows, so Y increases/grows Параллельный рост: оба движутся вместе 'As + S + V, so + V + S' (инверсия в второй клаузе — опционально) As access to higher education has expanded, so too has the value placed on postgraduate credentials — a development that risks simply displacing, rather than resolving, the bottleneck of credentialism.
Just as X, so Y / X, just as Y Аналогическое сравнение: Y работает по той же логике, что X 'Just as' + full clause + 'so' + full clause; или Y, just as X Just as the expansion of secondary education in the twentieth century failed to deliver the meritocratic society its proponents promised, so the current expansion of higher education shows every sign of reproducing a more credentialised version of the same hierarchies.
X no less than Y / X no more than Y Уравнивание или преуменьшение через отрицательное сравнение Parallel NPs or VPs The structural barriers to educational attainment are a consequence of policy choice no less than of resource constraint — and are therefore no less amenable to policy intervention.

⚡ The more X, the more Y: точность синтаксиса

Базовая форма: 'The more unequal a society, the lower the social mobility.'

Расширенная (Band 8): 'The more entrenched the socioeconomic stratification of schooling, the less likely it is that educational institutions will function as genuine engines of social mobility rather than as sophisticated instruments of its simulation.'

Анализ расширенной формы:

'The more entrenched' — comparative adj + complex NP: не просто 'unequal' а 'entrenched socioeconomic stratification'

'the less likely it is that' — it-extraposition: формально и гибко

'rather than as' — corrective clause добавляет второй элемент контраста

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

The greater the degree to which schooling systems sort pupils by socioeconomic background at an early age, the more deeply those systems embed the very inequalities they are ostensibly designed to address — and, crucially, the less likely it is that any subsequent policy intervention, however well-designed, will be capable of reversing outcomes that have been structurally determined long before formal education begins.

① The greater the degree to which — 'the greater' + complex NP with relative clause: akademische Tiefe

② the more deeply those systems embed — 'the more + adverb + V': parallel comparative clause

③ the very inequalities they are ostensibly designed to address — 'the very' для иронического контраста; sentence adverb 'ostensibly' (C13)

④ however well-designed — concessive clause (embedded): даже хорошая политика бессильна
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: The more education people get, the more they will earn money.

Правильно: The greater the level of educational attainment, the higher the probability of sustained upward occupational mobility — though the relationship is mediated by the type and prestige of credentials acquired.

Почему: Параллельная структура требует NP в обеих клаузах: 'The greater X, the higher Y'. 'The more education people get' — лучше: 'The higher the level of educational attainment'

Ошибка: As inequality grows, also social mobility decreases.

Правильно: As structural inequality deepens, so the capacity of educational systems to counteract its effects diminishes — suggesting that education and redistribution must be pursued as complementary rather than alternative strategies.

Почему: 'As X grows, also Y' — неправильно. Формула: 'As X grows, so Y grows/diminishes'. Инверсия 'so + V + S' — опциональна, но 'also' не работает в этой позиции
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Comparative Parallelism

✔ 'The greater X, the more Y' вместо 'If X increases, Y will increase' → немедленный Band 8 GRA: структура несёт аргумент

✔ 'Just as X, so Y' для исторической аналогии → сильнейший TA-инструмент: принцип из прошлого применяется к настоящему

✔ 'As X grows, so too does Y' — 'so too' → более формально, чем 'so also'; LR Band 7+
⚠ Снижает балл: 'The more X, the more Y' с нарушенным параллелизмом (разные синтаксические типы) — GRA снижается; строгость формы обязательна
📝 Задание 2 · Comparative Parallelism

1. Трансформируйте в параллельную сравнительную структуру: (a) 'If schools are more segregated, students from poor backgrounds do worse.' → The more... (b) 'When inequality increases, trust in institutions decreases.' → As X grows, so Y. (c) 'Credential inflation happened after secondary education expanded. It is happening again with higher education.' → Just as X, so Y.

2. Расширьте следующие базовые структуры до Band 8: (d) 'The more unequal a country, the less mobile it is.' → добавь complex NPs + hedging + concessive clause. (e) 'As access expands, quality decreases.' → добавь 'so too' + relative clause + qualification.

3. Напишите 2 предложения об образовании, используя 'the greater X, the more Y' и 'just as X, so Y'.

💭 Рефлексия: Почему 'the more X, the more Y' убедительнее, чем 'if X increases, Y increases'? В чём риторическая разница?

📗 Grammar 3 · Tricolon & Anaphoric Parallelism

Три элемента · Anaphora (повторение в начале) · Epistrophe (повторение в конце) · Chiasmus
🟣

Параллелизм не ограничивается двумя элементами.

В уровне C мы изучаем трёхэлементные структуры (tricolon) и риторические фигуры повторения, которые придают академическому тексту ритмическую убедительность.
Приём Структура Риторическая функция Пример
Tricolon (ascending) A, B, and C — каждый элемент сильнее предыдущего Кульминация: третий элемент — самый значимый Educational systems must address access, quality, and — above all — the structural reproduction of inequality that renders both access and quality insufficient without redistribution.
Tricolon (balanced) A, B, and C — все элементы равнозначны Полнота охвата: три аспекта одного явления Social mobility requires material resources, institutional opportunity, and the cultural capital that mediates between the two.
Anaphora Повторение слова/фразы в начале последовательных клауз Нагнетание, акцент, риторическое убеждение Not the absence of talent in disadvantaged communities, not the unwillingness of individuals to invest in their own development, but the systematic suppression of the conditions under which both talent and investment can yield returns.
Epistrophe Повторение слова/фразы в конце последовательных клауз Эхо: одна идея звучит настойчиво Education that reproduces hierarchy is not education. A meritocracy that perpetuates advantage is not a meritocracy. A mobility agenda that leaves structures intact is not a mobility agenda.
Chiasmus (A-B / B-A) Перекрёстная симметрия: первый элемент переворачивается во втором Антитетическая глубина: реверс выявляет парадокс We do not reform education to create a more equal society; we require a more equal society to permit education reform to succeed.
Parallelism + ellipsis Параллельные клаузы с пропущенным общим элементом Ритм + плотность одновременно (C16 + C17) Access has increased; quality has not; equity has diminished — and the gap between the three has widened with each successive reform cycle.

📐 Anaphora в академическом тексте: тонкая грань

Anaphora в риторике — повторение начальных слов для нагнетания. В академическом тексте она работает иначе: повторение структуры (не обязательно слова) создаёт ощущение систематического перечисления:

Слабая anaphora: 'Not talent is missing. Not ambition is missing. Not resources are missing.'

Сильная anaphora: 'Not the absence of talent among disadvantaged populations, not the insufficiency of individual motivation, but the structural organisation of opportunity itself — this is what educational reform must address.'

Разница: сильная форма использует сложные NP (C12) внутри каждого элемента, а не простые клаузы — это анафора плюс номинализация плюс фронтинг (C9).

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

It is not the intellectual capacity of students from disadvantaged backgrounds that is found wanting; not their willingness to invest effort in educational attainment; not, even, the dedication of the teachers who work within under-resourced institutions — but the structural architecture of a system that simultaneously claims to reward merit and systematically ensures that merit itself is unequally distributed.

① Not X; not Y; not, even, Z — tripartite anaphora with ascending intensity: 'even' marks the third as most surprising

② but the structural architecture of a system — the 'but' closes the anaphoric series: all three negations lead here

③ that simultaneously claims to... and systematically ensures that — parallel relative clauses with correlative contrast ('claims to X and ensures that not-X')

④ merit itself is unequally distributed — chiastic paradox: the criterion of meritocracy is itself unmeritocratically distributed
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Education needs more money, teachers should be better, and also schools need resources.

Правильно: Educational reform demands sustained financial investment, the systematic professionalisation of teaching, and a fundamental restructuring of the resource allocation mechanisms that currently perpetuate inequality.

Почему: Tricolon требует строгого параллелизма: все три элемента одного синтаксического типа. 'Money' (NP) / 'teachers should be better' (clause) / 'schools need resources' (clause) — три разных типа

Ошибка: We need access. We also need quality. We also need equity.

Правильно: What educational reform demands is not merely expanded access, nor improved quality in isolation, but the coordinated pursuit of both within a framework that places equity — not efficiency — at its normative centre.

Почему: Повторение 'We need' — слабая anaphora с личным местоимением. В академическом тексте: используй NP anaphora ('Not X, not Y, but Z') или 'What is required is not X but Y'
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Tricolon & Anaphora

✔ Tricolon с ascending intensity → финальный элемент получает максимальную риторическую силу (TA + CC)

✔ Anaphora с complex NPs (не простыми словами) → сигнал C12 + C17 = Band 8 GRA + LR

✔ Chiasmus для парадокса → демонстрирует концептуальную изощрённость; редко используется студентами ниже Band 8
⚠ Снижает балл: Триколон с нарушенным параллелизмом хуже, чем одно хорошо сформулированное предложение — форма должна работать, а не украшать
📝 Задание 3 · Tricolon & Anaphora

1. Создайте ascending tricolon для каждого тезиса: (a) 'Education systems need reform.' → три элемента, каждый конкретнее и важнее. (b) 'Social mobility has declined.' → три причины с ascending severity. (c) 'Governments must act.' → три уровня действия.

2. Напишите anaphoric sequence ('Not X, not Y, but Z') для: (d) Опровержение трёх ложных объяснений низкой мобильности. (e) Установление реальной причины неравенства в образовании.

3. Создайте chiasmus для: (f) Парадокс системы, которая обещает равенство, воспроизводя неравенство. (g) Связь между реформой и структурным изменением.

💭 Рефлексия: Прочитайте финальные параграфы любых двух статей из The Economist или аналогичного издания. Найдите tricolon или anaphora. Как они создают ощущение завершённости и убедительности?

📕 Grammar 4 · Rhetorical Architecture & Band 8 Upgrade

Как симметрия, контраст и ритм работают вместе в академическом аргументе
🟣

Параллельные структуры — это не украшение.

В Grammar 4 мы видим, как они становятся аргументативной архитектурой: симметрия формы создаёт симметрию мысли — и читатель это чувствует как убедительность.
Аргументативная задача Структура Пример Эффект
Опровергнуть ложную причину Not X but Y Educational failure is not a consequence of individual inadequacy but of the structural organisation of opportunity. Прямое, точное, необратимое опровержение
Показать двойной провал Neither X nor Y Neither expanded access nor improved pedagogical quality, in isolation, constitutes an adequate response to structural inequality. Отклоняет оба ложных решения одним движением
Установить пропорциональную зависимость The greater X, the more Y The greater the degree of early tracking in school systems, the more permanently those systems determine the life chances of children. Структура несёт причинность: форма = аргумент
Применить исторический принцип к настоящему Just as X, so Y Just as the grammar school system reproduced class advantage under the rhetoric of meritocracy, so selective university admissions systems risk doing today. Историческая аналогия как предупреждение
Создать кульминацию аргумента Ascending tricolon + anaphora Not the shortage of resources, not the absence of political will, but — above all — the structural indifference to the question of whose interests educational systems actually serve. Нагнетание + кульминация = максимальная риторическая сила

📐 Band 8 Conclusion: полная риторическая архитектура

Предложение 1 — Not X but Y (коррекция): What educational reform demands is not merely expanded access — though access matters — but a fundamental redistribution of the cultural, institutional, and material resources through which educational outcomes are determined.

Предложение 2 — The greater X, the more Y (причинность): The longer governments defer this redistribution, the more deeply entrenched the structural barriers to mobility become — and the more costly, in both social and economic terms, the eventual reckoning.

Предложение 3 — Just as X, so Y (исторический урок): Just as the expansion of formal education in the twentieth century failed to resolve the class divisions it promised to transcend, so the current expansion of credentialised access risks generating, at greater expense, a more elaborate version of the same outcome.

Предложение 4 — Ascending tricolon (кульминация): What is required is not reform of curricula, not expansion of institutions, but a serious political commitment to dismantling the structural conditions under which educational advantage is systematically reproduced across generations.
🚀 Band 8 Upgrade

Band 6: Education is important for social mobility. Governments should improve education and this will help poor people to succeed. It is not fair that rich people have better education.

Band 7: Educational inequality represents a significant barrier to social mobility. Governments must address both access and quality, as neither alone is sufficient to dismantle the structural barriers that disadvantaged students face.

Band 8: Educational inequality is not a consequence of differential effort or innate capacity — it is the systematic product of a social arrangement that distributes not merely material advantage but the cultural capital, the institutional familiarity, and the horizons of legitimate aspiration upon which educational success depends. Not more funding alone, not more qualified teachers alone, but a fundamental restructuring of the conditions under which educational outcomes are determined: this, and nothing less, is what equity in education demands.
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Full Rhetorical Architecture

✔ Not X but Y + The greater X, the more Y + Just as X, so Y + tricolon в одном заключении → Band 8 GRA + CC

✔ Параллельные структуры, несущие аргумент (не просто украшение) → высший TA: форма и содержание едины

✔ Ascending tricolon в финальном предложении → читатель чувствует завершённость и убедительность
⚠ Снижает балл: Параллельные структуры без смыслового содержания — пустая риторика; форма должна служить мысли, а не заменять её
📝 Задание 4 · Full Rhetorical Architecture

1. Напишите заключительный абзац (90–100 слов) об образовании и мобильности, используя схему: Not X but Y (предложение 1) → The greater X, the more Y (предложение 2) → ascending tricolon с anaphora (предложение 3–4). Обозначьте каждую структуру в скобках.

2. Перепишите следующий слабый вывод, применив все четыре риторических инструмента из Grammar 4: 'Education is the most important factor for social mobility. Governments should invest more in education. This will make society more equal. We need to do this soon.'

3. Проверьте свой абзац: (a) Параллелизм строгий — все элементы одного синтаксического типа? (b) Ascending tricolon — третий элемент действительно самый значимый? (c) Риторические структуры несут аргумент, а не просто украшают?

💭 Рефлексия: Прочитайте финальный параграф любого эссе, которое вы написали раньше. Добавьте один элемент риторического баланса. Как изменился эффект?

📖 Reading 1 · The Myth of Meritocracy

Академическое чтение: параллельные структуры как аргументативная архитектура
📖 The Myth of Meritocracy: Educational Systems, Cultural Capital, and the Reproduction of Privilege

It is not the intelligence of children from disadvantaged socioeconomic backgrounds that is measured and found wanting by elite educational systems; nor is it their motivation, their resilience, or their capacity for intellectual engagement. What is being measured — and what such systems are, in large part, designed to reward — is the possession of a particular form of cultural capital: the ease with which privileged children navigate institutional environments that have been constructed, often unconsciously, in their image. The greater the degree to which educational assessment rewards this tacit familiarity rather than the cognitive capacities it purports to measure, the more effectively the system reproduces the social hierarchies it claims to transcend.

Not merely the content of what is taught, but the manner in which it is taught, the expectations embedded in how learning is assessed, and the cultural assumptions that pervade the institutional environment — all of these systematically advantage students whose home environments have already prepared them for precisely this kind of engagement. As educational credentials have become both more widely distributed and more economically necessary, so too have the informal markers of cultural distinction that allow elite institutions and employers to differentiate among an increasingly credentialised applicant pool.

Just as the expansion of secondary education in the mid-twentieth century failed to deliver the classless society its proponents envisioned — producing instead a more credentialised version of the existing class hierarchy — so the expansion of higher education risks generating the same outcome at greater cost. Neither structural reform alone nor funding increases alone is adequate; what is required is, above all, a serious political commitment to challenging the cultural frameworks through which educational advantage is distributed, transmitted, and — crucially — legitimated as the natural reward of individual merit.

📝 Задания к Reading 1 — Анализ риторической архитектуры

1. Первый абзац открывается с трёхчастной anaphora ('It is not X; nor is it Y, Z; nor W'). Разберите её: сколько элементов, каков синтаксический тип каждого, и как 'What is being measured' закрывает серию?

2. 'The greater the degree to which educational assessment rewards... the more effectively the system reproduces...' — разберите полностью: синтаксические требования к обеим клаузам, роль 'rather than' внутри первой клаузы, и как структура сама по себе несёт аргумент.

3. Второй абзац содержит tricolon: 'Not merely the content of what is taught, but the manner in which it is taught, the expectations embedded in how learning is assessed, and the cultural assumptions that pervade the institutional environment'. Разберите: это ascending или balanced tricolon? Почему — и какой риторический эффект?

4. 'Just as the expansion of secondary education... so the expansion of higher education...' — исторический параллелизм. Что именно сопоставляется? Как 'at greater cost' добавляет иронию к параллели?

5. 'Neither structural reform alone nor funding increases alone is adequate' — почему 'is', а не 'are'? Найдите правило согласования 'neither X nor Y + verb'.

📚 Словарь урока — C1–C2 Academic Vocabulary (30 слов)

Слово Значение / Коллокация Пример
meritocracy меритократия; myth of meritocracy / meritocratic ideal / nominally meritocratic The meritocratic ideal holds that talent and effort, not birth, should determine life outcomes.
cultural capital культурный капитал; accumulate cultural capital / cultural capital deficit Cultural capital — the tacit knowledge and behavioural dispositions of dominant social groups — mediates educational outcomes.
social mobility социальная мобильность; upward/downward mobility / intergenerational mobility Intergenerational social mobility has declined markedly in most high-income economies since the 1980s.
stratification стратификация; social stratification / educational stratification / socioeconomic stratification The early stratification of schooling by ability tracking has profound and lasting effects on life chances.
credential диплом, удостоверение; academic credential / credential inflation / credentialism Credential inflation has diminished the labour market value of undergraduate degrees.
attainment успеваемость, достижение; educational attainment / attainment gap The attainment gap between socioeconomic groups persists despite decades of reform effort.
intergenerational межпоколенческий; intergenerational transmission / intergenerational inequality Intergenerational transmission of advantage is the primary mechanism through which inequality is reproduced.
aspiration стремление; intellectual aspiration / aspiration gap / horizon of aspiration The aspiration gap between advantaged and disadvantaged pupils emerges well before formal schooling begins.
pedagogy педагогика; pedagogical method / effective pedagogy / pedagogical reform Pedagogical methods that activate prior knowledge have shown strong effects on attainment in disadvantaged schools.
curriculum учебная программа; curriculum design / hidden curriculum / national curriculum The hidden curriculum communicates social norms and expectations alongside formal academic content.
tracking разделение по способностям; ability tracking / early tracking / track assignment Early ability tracking has been shown to amplify socioeconomic differences in educational outcomes.
reproduction воспроизводство; social reproduction / reproduction of inequality / cultural reproduction Educational systems function as mechanisms for the intergenerational reproduction of social advantage.
equity справедливость (в образовании); educational equity / pursue equity / equity vs equality Equity in education requires differential resource allocation to compensate for differential starting conditions.
hegemony гегемония; cultural hegemony / dominant hegemony Gramsci's concept of cultural hegemony illuminates how dominant social values are transmitted through educational institutions.
dispositional диспозиционный; dispositional advantage / dispositional preparation Elite educational environments reward dispositional characteristics that are systematically instilled in privileged households.
habitus хабитус (Бурдьё); class habitus / habitus formation Bourdieu's concept of habitus refers to the durable dispositions through which class experience is embodied and reproduced.
institutional институциональный; institutional environment / institutional reform Institutional environments that assume a particular kind of cultural preparation systematically disadvantage those who lack it.
aspire стремиться; aspire to / aspiration level / bounded aspiration Students from disadvantaged backgrounds frequently develop bounded aspirations that reflect perceived rather than actual barriers.
perpetuate увековечивать; perpetuate inequality / perpetuate advantage Selective educational systems perpetuate socioeconomic advantage across generations.
remediation восстановительное обучение; remediation programme / need for remediation Remediation programmes at university level address the educational deficits accumulated by disadvantaged students.
differentiate дифференцировать; differentiate among applicants / differentiated instruction Employers use informal cultural cues to differentiate among applicants with equivalent formal credentials.
norm норма; social norm / institutional norm / normative expectation The norms of elite educational institutions reflect the cultural expectations of dominant social groups.
exclusion исключение; social exclusion / educational exclusion / mechanisms of exclusion Mechanisms of educational exclusion operate through both formal structures and informal cultural processes.
discourse дискурс; educational discourse / dominant discourse / policy discourse The dominant discourse of meritocracy legitimates inequality by attributing it to differential individual effort.
reform реформа; structural reform / educational reform / reform agenda Educational reform that focuses on access without addressing structural inequality is likely to prove insufficient.
professionalism профессионализм; teaching professionalism / professional development The professionalisation of teaching is a necessary but insufficient condition for improving educational outcomes.
cohort когорта; birth cohort / student cohort / cohort effect Cohort studies have demonstrated the persistence of socioeconomic differentials in educational attainment.
intervention вмешательство; early intervention / policy intervention / targeted intervention Early childhood intervention programmes have shown the most robust evidence of long-term attainment effects.
accountability подотчётность; school accountability / teacher accountability / accountability framework Accountability frameworks that focus solely on test scores may generate perverse incentives for schools.
cognitive когнитивный; cognitive development / cognitive outcomes / cognitive capacity Socioeconomic inequality has measurable effects on cognitive development from the earliest years of life.

📖 Reading 2 · What Educational Reform Cannot Do Alone

Параллельные структуры и ограничения аргумента
📖 What Educational Reform Cannot Do Alone: The Structural Limits of Meritocratic Policy

It is one of the characteristic features of contemporary policy discourse on social mobility that educational reform is simultaneously treated as the primary mechanism for addressing structural inequality and subjected to criticism for its persistent failure to deliver the outcomes it promises. This apparent paradox is, however, less contradictory than it appears: the more that education is asked to compensate for structural inequalities that originate outside the school system, the less adequate any purely educational response will prove to be. Not merely is this a structural limitation of educational policy; it is, more fundamentally, a symptom of a political unwillingness to address the social and economic conditions that make educational advantage heritable in the first place.

Just as it would be unreasonable to expect medicine to compensate for poverty-driven ill health without simultaneously addressing its social determinants, so it is unreasonable to expect educational systems to generate social mobility while the wider social environment systematically reproduces the conditions that render such mobility structurally improbable. Neither expanding university access nor improving school quality, however necessary both may be, is capable of resolving an inequality that is reproduced not merely through educational institutions but through the entire complex of housing markets, labour markets, cultural industries, and social networks through which advantage is transmitted and consolidated.

What is required, therefore, is not a choice between educational reform and structural economic redistribution — the former without the latter is insufficient, the latter without the former is incomplete — but the coordinated and sustained pursuit of both within a political framework that refuses to accept the existing distribution of advantage as either natural or permanent. The greater the political commitment to such a framework, the more likely it is that educational reform will generate the outcomes its advocates promise; the weaker that commitment, the more certainly educational reform will reproduce — at public expense and with the consent of its beneficiaries — precisely the hierarchies it purports to dismantle.

📝 Задания к Reading 2 — Риторический анализ

1. Первый абзац содержит 'the more that education is asked to compensate..., the less adequate any purely educational response will prove to be.' Это не стандартная 'the more X, the more Y' — здесь 'the more that + clause, the less + NP'. Разберите синтаксис: можно ли так строить? Что меняется семантически?

2. 'Just as it would be unreasonable to expect medicine to compensate... so it is unreasonable to expect educational systems...' — это аналогия через 'just as... so'. Насколько эта аналогия сильна? Какие возражения можно ей предъявить — и как автор мог бы их предвосхитить?

3. Третий абзац содержит скрытый chiasmus: 'the former without the latter is insufficient, the latter without the former is incomplete'. Разберите: A без B = X; B без A = Y. Что делает это именно chiasmus, а не просто параллелизмом?

4. Финальное предложение: 'The greater the political commitment..., the more likely...; the weaker that commitment, the more certainly...' — двойная 'the more X, the more Y' с антитетическим контрастом. Как вторая половина переворачивает первую? Какой риторический эффект создаёт эта структура в конце эссе?

5. Напишите ответный абзац (80–90 слов), полемизирующий с позицией автора, используя минимум три параллельных структуры из урока. Затем объясните риторический выбор каждой.

✍️ Writing Task 1 · Bar Chart Description

Параллельные структуры в описании данных: сравнение через симметрию
✍️ Writing Task 1 · Bar Chart (170 слов, цель: Band 7.5)

Задание:

The bar chart below shows the percentage of students from the highest and lowest socioeconomic quintiles who completed upper secondary education and who enrolled in higher education in six countries in 2022. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

📌 Model Opening — Band 8:

The data reveal a pattern of consistent and, in several cases, striking socioeconomic divergence in educational participation: not merely at the point of upper secondary completion — where the gap between the highest and lowest quintiles averages 22 percentage points across the six countries — but, more consequentially, at the point of higher education enrolment, where the differential widens to an average of 34 points, suggesting that each successive educational transition amplifies rather than diminishes the initial disadvantage.

① not merely at the point of X... but, more consequentially, at the point of Y — 'not merely... but' parallel PP structure

② amplifies rather than diminishes — correlative contrast: 'rather than' shows the system worsens, not corrects

③ the initial disadvantage — nominal substitute (C16): avoids repeating the full description

④ the differential widens to an average of 34 points — precise academic register: 'differential' not 'gap difference'

👁 What the Examiner Sees:

TA (7–8): Два уровня перехода сравниваются; тенденция усиления неравенства выявлена

CC (7–8): 'Not merely X but Y' создаёт структурную прогрессию от secondary к tertiary

LR (7–8): socioeconomic quintiles, differential, enrolment, amplifies, participation — точный академический регистр

GRA (7–8): 'Not merely... but' + 'rather than' + participial clause в одном сложном предложении

📐 Структура эссе:

Overview (2 пр.): 'Not merely X but Y': двухуровневая структура. 'The greater the socioeconomic gap, the more pronounced...'

Body 1 (3–4 пр.): 'Both X and Y show...': страны с наименьшим расхождением. Comparative parallelism.

Body 2 (3–4 пр.): 'Just as upper secondary gaps are large, so higher education gaps are larger still.' Ascending tricolon для итогов.

✍️ Writing Task 2 · Problem-Solution Essay

Риторическая архитектура в аргументативном эссе
✍️ Writing Task 2 · Problem-Solution Essay (270 слов, цель: Band 7.5)

Задание:

In many countries, students from lower socioeconomic backgrounds are significantly less likely to complete higher education than their more advantaged peers, despite having similar academic ability. What are the main reasons for this, and what measures could be taken to address the problem?

📌 Model Opening — Band 8:

The underrepresentation of students from lower socioeconomic backgrounds in higher education is attributable not merely to financial barriers — though such barriers are real and substantial — but, more fundamentally, to the systematic attenuation of educational aspiration that occurs when individuals are raised in environments that have been, structurally and culturally, organised around the assumption that higher education is not for people like them.

① not merely to X — though X is real — but, more fundamentally, to Y — 'not merely... but' with embedded concession

② the systematic attenuation of educational aspiration — complex nominalised NP (C12): 'aspiration is attenuated'

③ environments that have been, structurally and culturally, organised around the assumption that — parallel adverbs + complex relative

④ higher education is not for people like them — indirect discourse: the internalised voice of structural exclusion

👁 What the Examiner Sees:

TA (7–8): Причины структурные (не индивидуальные); меры адресуют структуру, а не симптомы

CC (7–8): Not merely X but Y (причины) → Neither X alone nor Y alone (меры) → ascending tricolon (выводы)

LR (7–8): attenuation, aspiration, credential, intergenerational, structural — C2 лексика

GRA (7–8): 'Not merely... but' + embedded concession + complex NP + 'neither X nor Y' в разных частях

📐 Структура эссе:

Intro (2 пр.): Not merely X but Y для постановки проблемы. Тезис через 'what is required is not X but Y'.

Reasons (3–4 пр.): The greater X, the more Y для механизма передачи неравенства. Tricolon для трёх уровней барьеров.

Solutions (3–4 пр.): Neither X alone nor Y alone. Both X and Y must be pursued. Just as X worked in context A, so Y could work here.

Conclusion (2 пр.): Ascending anaphora + chiasmus финального тезиса.

🎓 Урок C17 завершён!

✅ Correlative conjunctions: Not only X but Y / Not X but Y / Not merely X but Y / Not so much X as Y / Both X and Y / Neither X nor Y — каждый создаёт разные риторические весы

✅ Comparative parallelism: The more/greater X, the more Y · As X grows, so Y · Just as X, so Y · X no less than Y — форма несёт причинность и аналогию

✅ Tricolon & anaphora: ascending tricolon / balanced tricolon / anaphoric series / epistrophe / chiasmus — ритм как аргумент

✅ Rhetorical Architecture: четыре структуры в одном заключительном абзаце создают профессиональную убедительность

✅ Band 8 Parallelism = строгий синтаксический параллелизм + смысловая необходимость формы + риторический эффект

💡 Central Question — Ответ:

Симметрия делает аргумент убедительнее по одной причине: когда форма отражает структуру мысли, читатель не просто понимает — он воспринимает мысль как неизбежную. 'Not X but Y' не просто опровергает X — структура опровержения встроена в форму предложения. 'The more X, the more Y' не просто утверждает корреляцию — она демонстрирует её через синтаксическую симметрию. Именно это и есть риторическая сила параллельных структур: мысль и форма — одно.

Урок C18 · Upper-Intermediate / Advanced

Extended Metaphor & Academic Imagery

Как управляемая метафора делает абстрактный аргумент конкретным?

Тематика: Технологии и общество · Словарь: C1–C2 Academic · Writing: Opinion Essay

💥 Урочный крючок — Зачем это нужно?

Три описания одного явления — алгоритмических рекомендательных систем:

Буквальное: Recommendation algorithms analyse user behaviour and show content that maximises engagement.

Одна метафора: Recommendation algorithms function as a mirror, reflecting users' existing preferences back at them.

Расширенная метафора: Recommendation algorithms function not as windows — through which users might encounter the unfamiliar — but as mirrors, reflecting existing preferences back with increasing precision; and like all mirrors, they offer not the world as it is but the world as the viewer has already decided it should appear.

Буквальное описание передаёт факт. Одна метафора добавляет образ. Расширенная метафора строит концептуальную модель: читатель не просто понимает алгоритм — он видит его в новом свете. Именно это и делает extended metaphor одним из самых мощных инструментов академической риторики.

📘 Grammar 1 · Метафора в академическом дискурсе: типология и функция

Dead metaphors · Conventional metaphors · Fresh metaphors · Conceptual metaphors
🟣

Студенты уровня B избегают метафор в IELTS — боясь, что это 'не академично'.

Студенты уровня C знают: академический текст насыщен метафорами — просто правильно управляемыми. Цель урока — научиться использовать их как аналитический инструмент, а не как украшение.
Тип метафоры Определение Академическая функция Пример в теме технологий
Dead metaphor Метафора, переставшая восприниматься как метафора Составляет большую часть академического словаря; невидима, но функциональна 'Address a problem', 'underpin an argument', 'erode trust', 'navigate regulations' — всё это мёртвые метафоры
Conventional metaphor Устойчивая метафора области знания Структурирует понимание концепции в данной дисциплине 'The information ecosystem', 'the digital marketplace', 'the attention economy' — метафоры, ставшие техническими терминами
Fresh / novel metaphor Новая метафора, создающая новый взгляд Делает абстрактное конкретным; запоминается; сигнализирует о концептуальной изощрённости 'Algorithms function not as windows but as mirrors' — свежая метафора для filter bubble
Conceptual / structural metaphor Метафора, через которую строится целая концепция Организует понимание всего явления: источник → цель TECHNOLOGY IS A TOOL (нейтральная) vs TECHNOLOGY IS AN ECOLOGY (органическая, взаимозависимая) vs TECHNOLOGY IS A MARKET (транзакционная, ценностная)
Extended metaphor Метафора, последовательно разворачиваемая через несколько предложений Строит концептуальную модель; позволяет исследовать все измерения явления Алгоритм как зеркало → зеркало искажает → зеркальный зал → эффект эхо-камеры

⚡ Почему 'technology is a tool' — слабее, чем 'technology is an ecology'?

Метафора инструмента: Technology is a tool — подразумевает пассивность, нейтральность, полный человеческий контроль. Инструмент делает то, что ему говорят. Этот взгляд не позволяет увидеть, что технология структурирует поведение, формирует ценности, создаёт зависимости.

Метафора экологии: Technology is an ecology — подразумевает взаимозависимость, эволюцию, неконтролируемые последствия, адаптацию. Экология не нейтральна: она формирует тех, кто в ней живёт.

Выбор концептуальной метафоры — это политический и аналитический выбор. Он предопределяет, какие вопросы кажутся разумными, а какие — абсурдными.
Концептуальная метафора Что она делает видимым Что она скрывает Академический контекст
Technology as TOOL Человеческий контроль, инструментальность Структурирующее воздействие на поведение, ценности, зависимости Дискурс нейтральности технологий; 'guns don't kill people'
Technology as ECOLOGY Взаимозависимость, эволюция, непредсказуемость Намеренное проектирование, корпоративная ответственность Дискурс сложных систем; теория сетей
Technology as ARCHITECTURE Дизайн среды формирует поведение Агентность пользователей, возможность сопротивления Выбор архитектуры (C9); «nudge» теория (C13)
Technology as MARKET Конкуренция, стимулы, рациональные агенты Власть, неравенство, монополизация, общественные блага Экономический детерминизм; дискурс регулирования
Technology as MIRROR Отражение, нарциссизм, искажение Активное формирование, а не пассивное отражение Критика filter bubble; epistemicharm
Technology as INFRASTRUCTURE Невидимость, встроенность, зависимость Сознательный выбор дизайна, возможность реконструкции Политэкономия платформ; digital commons

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

The dominant metaphor through which digital platforms have been conceptualised — both in popular discourse and in much of the regulatory literature — is that of a marketplace: a neutral space in which buyers and sellers of information, attention, and goods freely exchange their respective surpluses. This framing, while rhetorically convenient, systematically obscures the degree to which the architecture of such platforms does not merely facilitate exchange but actively shapes the preferences, behaviours, and epistemic orientations of those who participate within it.

① The dominant metaphor through which — nominalisaion of 'people conceptualise X through metaphor'

② that of a marketplace — nominal substitute (C16): 'that' replaces 'the dominant metaphor'

③ a neutral space in which buyers and sellers... freely exchange their respective surpluses — extends the marketplace metaphor: each word activates a different dimension

④ This framing, while rhetorically convenient — comment clause (C13) + admission of rhetorical utility before critique
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Technology is like a double-edged sword which can be good or bad.

Правильно: Digital technology functions less as a neutral instrument — the double-edged sword of popular discourse — than as an architecture of incentives that systematically shapes the behaviours it purports merely to facilitate.

Почему: 'Double-edged sword' — мёртвая клише-метафора, не добавляет аналитической ценности. В академическом тексте: либо развивай метафору концептуально, либо не используй её

Ошибка: Social media is a monster that is eating our brains.

Правильно: Social media platforms are more accurately conceptualised as attentional architectures — systems specifically engineered to maximise the duration and intensity of cognitive engagement, regardless of its quality or consequences.

Почему: Эмоциональная метафора ('monster', 'eating our brains') — не академический регистр. Замени на концептуальную метафору с аналитической нагрузкой
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Metaphor in Academic Discourse

✔ Conventional metaphor ('attention economy', 'information ecosystem') — корректное использование дисциплинарного языка (LR Band 7+)

✔ Fresh metaphor с аналитическим развитием → демонстрирует концептуальную изощрённость (TA Band 8)

✔ Critique of another's metaphor ('this framing... systematically obscures') → показывает метауровень аналитического мышления
⚠ Снижает балл: Эмоциональная метафора без аналитической нагрузки ('is a monster', 'is destroying society') — нарушение регистра (C15) и снижение TA
📝 Задание 1 · Metaphor Typology

1. Определите тип метафоры и её аналитическую функцию: (a) 'The algorithm amplifies harmful content.' (b) 'The attention economy treats human consciousness as a commodity.' (c) 'Social media platforms function as echo chambers that prevent genuine democratic deliberation.' (d) 'Artificial intelligence is a genie that cannot be put back in the bottle.'

2. Выберите концептуальную метафору для описания интернета и объясните: (e) что она делает видимым, (f) что скрывает, (g) какой политический или аналитический вывод она предрасполагает.

3. Напишите 2–3 предложения о влиянии социальных сетей на демократию, используя одну конвенциональную метафору и одну свежую — без клише.

💭 Рефлексия: Почему выбор метафоры — это аналитический и политический выбор, а не просто стилистический?

📙 Grammar 2 · Building an Extended Metaphor: как разворачивать метафору

Source domain → Target domain mapping · Entailments · Limits of the metaphor
🟣

Одна метафора — это образ.

Extended metaphor — это концептуальная модель. Она разворачивается через несколько предложений, последовательно используя разные аспекты исходной области (source domain) для освещения целевой области (target domain).

📐 Структура extended metaphor: source → target mapping

Шаг 1 — Установи source domain: 'The digital public sphere functions, in many respects, as an ecosystem.'

Шаг 2 — Развивай entailments (следствия метафоры): 'Like any ecosystem, it is structured by competitive dynamics — some organisms [platforms] consuming the resources [attention] upon which others depend.'

Шаг 3 — Углубляй аналитическую ценность: 'The introduction of a dominant predator [monopolistic platform] into an ecosystem that previously supported diverse species [independent media] tends to reduce biodiversity — and, with it, the resilience of the system as a whole.'

Шаг 4 — Признай ограничения метафоры: 'The ecosystem metaphor, however, risks naturalising what is in fact a product of deliberate regulatory choices: ecosystems evolve; platforms are designed.'
Source domain Target domain Ключевые entailments Аналитическая ценность Ограничение
Ecosystem / ecology Digital media environment Biodiversity, predation, resilience, collapse Позволяет говорить о системных рисках, монополии, устойчивости Натурализует: экосистемы не проектируются людьми
Architecture / infrastructure Platform design Built environment shapes behaviour, invisible, hard to change 'Choice architecture' (C13); дизайн как политика; lock-in эффект Скрывает агентность пользователей
Mirror / reflection Personalisation algorithms Reflection vs distortion, narcissism, self-reinforcement Filter bubble, echo chamber, epistemic closure Показывает пассивность алгоритма, скрывает его активную роль
Marketplace / economy Attention and data Supply/demand, pricing, commodity, monopoly 'Attention economy' (Goldhaber); platform economics Натурализует commodification сознания
Public square / agora Social media Civic participation, voice, exclusion, cacophony Нормативное измерение; standards of civility Идеализирует Agora; классическая Agora была эксклюзивной
Nervous system Internet infrastructure Signal transmission, reflex, paralysis, vulnerability Уязвимость, критическая зависимость, кибербезопасность Органицистская: скрывает политическое измерение контроля

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

If the early internet was imagined as a public square — open, egalitarian, governed by the norms of civil discourse — then what has emerged in its place is something closer to a private shopping mall: a space that mimics the visual grammar of public life while remaining, in every legally and architecturally significant respect, the exclusive property of its owners, who reserve the right to determine who speaks, what is displayed, and under what conditions participation is permitted.

① imagined as a public square — establishes the first term of the extended metaphor; 'imagined' signals it was always a metaphor

② what has emerged in its place is something closer to a private shopping mall — contrast metaphor: same visual grammar, different ownership structure

③ mimics the visual grammar of public life — 'visual grammar' is itself a dead metaphor activated freshly here

④ who reserve the right to determine who speaks, what is displayed, and under what conditions — tricolon (C17) within the extended metaphor
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: The internet is like a jungle where only the strongest survive.

Правильно: The digital media landscape increasingly resembles an ecological monoculture — one in which a small number of dominant platform species have displaced the diverse and competitive environment that once characterised the early web.

Почему: 'Jungle where only the strongest survive' — Social Darwinism клише. Замени на конкретную экологическую аналогию ('monoculture', 'displacement', 'species diversity') с аналитической нагрузкой

Ошибка: Technology is a mirror but also it is a window and a door at the same time.

Правильно: Whether digital technology functions primarily as a mirror — reflecting and reinforcing existing dispositions — or as a window — opening perspectives that would otherwise remain inaccessible — depends, I would contend, less on the technology itself than on the regulatory and design choices that govern its deployment.

Почему: Смешение трёх метафор в одном предложении без структуры — хаотично. Extended metaphor требует последовательности: одна метафора за раз, с аналитическим развитием
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Extended Metaphor

✔ Source → target mapping с 3–4 entailments → TA Band 8: автор строит концептуальную модель, не просто описывает

✔ Признание ограничений метафоры ('the metaphor risks naturalising...') → показывает метааналитическое мышление

✔ Two contrasting metaphors (public square → shopping mall) → TA: демонстрирует изменение через образ
⚠ Снижает балл: Расширенная метафора без аналитической нагрузки — красивая пустота; каждый шаг метафоры должен освещать новое измерение аргумента
📝 Задание 2 · Building an Extended Metaphor

1. Постройте extended metaphor для следующих концепций, используя четыре шага (establish → entailments → deepen → limit): (a) Алгоритмические рекомендации как зеркало. (b) Социальные сети как публичная площадь. (c) Данные как ресурс (нефть / вода — выбери и объясни).

2. Для каждой метафоры ответь: (d) Что она делает видимым? (e) Что она скрывает или искажает? (f) Когда её ограничения становятся аналитически опасными?

3. Напишите абзац (70–80 слов) о влиянии алгоритмов на демократию, используя одну extended metaphor из 3–4 предложений. Заверши признанием её ограничения.

💭 Рефлексия: Когда метафора помогает аргументу — а когда она его заменяет? Как понять разницу?

📗 Grammar 3 · Controlled Imagery in Formal Prose

Analogy · Thought experiment · Concrete illustration · Qualifying the image
🟣

Extended metaphor — это образная концептуальная модель.

В Grammar 3 мы изучаем более широкий арсенал образных инструментов, которые в академическом тексте делают абстрактное конкретным — без потери формального регистра.
Инструмент Функция Синтаксическая форма Пример
Analogy (explicit) Поясняет принцип через хорошо известную область 'X is analogous to Y in that...' / 'Like Y, X...' / 'In much the same way that Y, X...' In much the same way that a river constrained to a single channel flows faster and more destructively than one dispersed across a floodplain, attention concentrated by algorithmic recommendation produces more intense but less diverse epistemic outcomes.
Thought experiment Проверяет принцип через мысленный сценарий (C14) 'Imagine a context in which...' / 'Consider a scenario where...' / 'Suppose that...' Consider a scenario in which every newspaper reader received a personalised edition containing only articles likely to confirm their existing views — the epistemic consequences would be immediately obvious. This is, in essence, the situation that algorithmic content curation has created.
Concrete illustration Делает абстрактное ощутимым через конкретный пример 'Consider the case of...' / 'Take, for instance,...' / 'A striking example is afforded by...' A striking example is afforded by the documented tendency of recommendation algorithms to amplify emotionally provocative content: not because such content is more true or more important, but because it generates more engagement — and engagement, in the attention economy, is the currency through which platform value is measured.
Spatial / visual image Создаёт пространственную метафору для абстрактного явления Formal use of 'landscape', 'terrain', 'architecture', 'sphere' The information landscape that has emerged under algorithmic curation is not a flat plain of equally accessible content but a deeply contoured terrain in which certain ideas occupy commanding heights while others remain in permanent shadow.
Qualifying the image Признаёт ограничения образа, сохраняя его ценность 'The analogy is imperfect in one important respect...' / 'The metaphor has its limits...' / 'It would be misleading to press this analogy too far...' The analogy between platform monopoly and industrial monopoly is suggestive but imperfect: unlike steel or railways, the 'product' that digital platforms monopolise is not a physical commodity but the structure of human attention and the architecture of epistemic access.

⚡ Qualifying the image: обязательный шаг

В академическом тексте использование аналогии или метафоры без признания её ограничений — это риторически слабая позиция: читатель-эксперт сразу видит, где аналогия ломается, и может использовать это против аргумента.

Слабо: 'Algorithms are like addictive drugs — and therefore should be regulated like them.'

Сильно: 'The analogy between algorithmic engagement mechanics and addiction pharmacology is, it must be acknowledged, imprecise — yet it captures something important: both operate through reward circuitry in ways that can override deliberate choice. The analogy's utility lies not in its precision but in its illumination of a governance gap: we regulate substances that exploit reward circuitry; we do not yet systematically regulate software that does the same.'

⭐ Model Sentence — Band 8

The information landscape that algorithmic curation has produced is not, as is sometimes suggested, a simple echo chamber — a metaphor that implies a static enclosure from which no external sound penetrates. It is, rather, something more dynamic and more troubling: a finely tuned acoustic environment in which certain frequencies are systematically amplified, others attenuated, and the listener is gradually habituated to the resulting soundscape as if it were the natural distribution of signal and noise.

① not, as is sometimes suggested, a simple echo chamber — acknowledges the common metaphor and immediately extends it

② a metaphor that implies a static enclosure — appositive NP critiquing the source domain of the existing metaphor

③ a finely tuned acoustic environment — new extended metaphor: moves from 'echo' to 'acoustic engineering'

④ amplified / attenuated / habituated — three verbs from acoustic/psychological domain: consistent metaphor extension
❌ Типичные ошибки

Ошибка: Technology is destroying society, just like the Black Death destroyed medieval Europe.

Правильно: The disruption wrought by algorithmic media upon established information ecosystems is, in some respects, analogous to the disruption caused by the printing press — though the analogy should not be pressed too far, since the printing press, unlike algorithmic curation, did not actively select which ideas would be amplified.

Почему: Аналогия с Чёрной смертью эмоциональна и аналитически бесполезна. Продуктивная аналогия должна освещать механизм: printing press → algorithmic curation = disruption of information ecosystem, но с важным различием

Ошибка: If we think about it like a river, technology flows and sometimes floods.

Правильно: Technology diffusion can be usefully conceptualised as a hydrological process: new platforms spread through social networks along paths of least resistance, pooling in communities of shared interest and only subsequently — if at all — breaching the embankments of institutional constraint that regulation attempts to construct.

Почему: 'Flows and sometimes floods' — слишком поверхностное развитие. Extended metaphor требует развёртывания через конкретные entailments: 'paths of least resistance', 'pooling', 'embankments of constraint' — каждый аспект метафоры несёт аналитическую нагрузку
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Controlled Imagery

✔ Concrete illustration ('a striking example is afforded by') → TA: конкретный пример заземляет абстрактный аргумент

✔ Qualifying the image ('the analogy is imperfect in one respect') → TA + GRA: сложность мышления, а не упрощение

✔ Spatial image ('information landscape', 'commanding heights') → LR Band 8: мёртвые метафоры оживляются в системе
⚠ Снижает балл: Аналогия, которую можно легко опровергнуть, слабее, чем отсутствие аналогии; всегда признавай ограничения образа

1. Постройте analogy с обязательным qualifying: (a) Алгоритмическая цензура и традиционная редакторская политика. (b) Монополизация внимания и монополизация воды. (c) Распространение дезинформации и распространение вируса.

2. Напишите thought experiment (2–3 предложения) для проверки принципа: (d) 'Algorithmic curation creates epistemic inequality.' (e) 'Data collection is a form of surveillance that would be intolerable in physical space.'

3. Используйте spatial image для описания: (f) Информационного пространства после алгоритмической кураторики. (g) Баланса власти между платформами и регуляторами.

💭 Рефлексия: Чем аналогия отличается от extended metaphor? Когда одна сильнее другой — и почему?

📕 Grammar 4 · Integration & Band 8 Upgrade

Метафора как аргументативная архитектура: от образа к аналитическому выводу
🟣

Метафора в академическом тексте — не украшение и не иллюстрация.

В Grammar 4 мы видим, как она становится структурой аргумента: каждый шаг метафоры соответствует шагу аналитической мысли.
Аргументативный шаг Метафорический инструмент Синтаксическая форма Пример
Установить концептуальную рамку Введение source domain 'X can be usefully conceptualised as...' / 'The dominant framing treats X as...' Digital platforms have been conceptualised — in regulatory discourse, at least — as neutral conduits for user-generated content, analogous to telecommunications infrastructure.
Развернуть entailments Последовательное расширение метафоры Series of parallel structures within source domain (C17) Like telecommunications infrastructure, they carry content they do not create; like publishers, they profit from content they do not pay for; unlike either, they actively shape the ranking, visibility, and emotional framing of what they transmit.
Обнажить пределы конкурирующей метафоры Critique of the source domain of a rival metaphor 'This framing, however, systematically obscures...' / 'The metaphor breaks down at precisely the point where...' The conduit metaphor, however, breaks down at precisely the point where it would be most useful: a conduit transmits without selection; an algorithm curates, amplifies, and suppresses.
Предложить альтернативную концептуальную метафору Introduce rival source domain 'A more apt analogy would be...' / 'It is more illuminating to conceptualise X as...' A more analytically productive framing would treat digital platforms not as conduits but as curators — and, as any museum visitor knows, curation is never neutral.
Признать ограничение новой метафоры Qualify and close 'The curator metaphor, in turn, has its limits...' / 'It would be misleading to press this analogy too far...' The curator analogy, in turn, risks underplaying the scale and automation of algorithmic selection — no human curator operates at the speed of two billion decisions per second.

📐 Band 8 Metaphorical Argument: полная архитектура

Предложение 1 — Установить рамку: The regulatory framework governing digital platforms has, since its inception, been structured around a single foundational metaphor: the platform as a neutral conduit, carrying content without selecting it, transmitting without endorsing it.

Предложение 2 — Развернуть entailments: This framing carries with it a set of legally and politically consequential entailments: as conduits, platforms cannot be held responsible for what flows through them; as neutral infrastructure, they require no editorial oversight; as passive intermediaries, they deserve the liability protections that were designed for telephone exchanges.

Предложение 3 — Обнажить предел: The metaphor, however, collapses the moment one asks what a telephone exchange would look like if it decided, algorithmically, which calls to connect based on which conversations were most likely to generate additional calls.

Предложение 4 — Альтернатива + квалификация: If platforms must be compared to anything, it is not to telephone exchanges but to editors — active selectors of what an audience sees, hears, and, ultimately, believes — though even this analogy understates the scale at which algorithmic editorial decisions are made.
🚀 Band 8 Upgrade

Band 6: Social media companies are not responsible for what users post. They are just a platform like a telephone company. But they do control what people see, so maybe they should be regulated.

Band 7: Social media platforms operate under a legal framework that treats them as neutral intermediaries. However, since they actively curate content through algorithmic selection, this framework may be inadequate.

Band 8: The legal architecture governing digital platforms rests upon a foundational analogy that has ceased to describe the reality it purports to govern: the platform as neutral conduit. A telephone exchange transmits without selecting; an algorithm curates without disclosing. A publisher chooses what to amplify; so, at two billion decisions per second, does an algorithmic feed. The conduit metaphor, far from being a neutral description, is a regulatory choice — and one whose consequences, as the epistemic landscape of contemporary democracy attests, have been considerable.
🔵 Examiner's Eye — Full Metaphorical Architecture

✔ Introduce metaphor → extend entailments → expose limits → propose alternative → qualify: five-step Band 8 pattern

✔ Competing metaphors (conduit vs curator vs editor) → TA: démonstration that framing is itself an argument

✔ Thought experiment within metaphor ('what would a telephone exchange look like if...') → GRA + TA: C14 + C18 synthesis
⚠ Снижает балл: Метафора без аналитического вывода — иллюстрация без аргумента; всегда замыкай метафору на тезис
📝 Задание 4 · Full Metaphorical Argument

1. Постройте полный метафорический аргумент (90–100 слов) по пятишаговой схеме о данных как нефти или как воде. Обозначьте каждый шаг в скобках.

2. Перепишите следующий абзац, добавив extended metaphor с qualifying: 'Algorithms are used by social media companies to decide what content users see. This affects what people believe. It can create filter bubbles. This is a serious problem for democracy.'

3. Проверьте свою метафору: (a) Каждый entailment несёт аналитическую нагрузку — а не просто украшает? (b) Ограничение метафоры признано явно? (c) Метафора замыкается на тезис — или повисает в воздухе?

💭 Рефлексия: Прочитайте любую академическую статью о технологиях и найдите в ней скрытую концептуальную метафору. Какие аналитические выводы она предрасполагает? Какие закрывает?

📖 Reading 1 · The Architecture of Attention

Академическое чтение: extended metaphor в критическом анализе технологий
📖 The Architecture of Attention: Algorithmic Curation, Epistemic Inequality, and the Metaphorical Framing of Platform Power

The metaphors through which we conceptualise digital media are not merely rhetorical conveniences — they are, in a substantive sense, constitutive of the regulatory and political frameworks within which platforms operate. The dominant metaphor — the platform as a neutral conduit or marketplace for ideas — has, since the early 1990s, provided the conceptual infrastructure for a regulatory approach premised on minimal intervention, maximum autonomy, and the assumption that the free flow of information is, in itself, epistemically beneficial. This framing, while rhetorically powerful, rests upon an analogy that has become increasingly difficult to sustain: a marketplace in which the prices are set not by competitive dynamics but by engagement metrics engineered to maximise addiction is not a free market in any meaningful sense.

A more analytically productive metaphor — one that is gaining traction in both academic and regulatory discourse — conceptualises digital platforms not as neutral conduits but as curators of the information environment: active selectors, rankers, and amplifiers whose editorial choices, however automated, produce structural effects on the epistemic landscape that are no less consequential for being the product of an algorithm rather than a human editor. Crucially, curators are not neutral. Every museum exhibition, every curated playlist, every algorithmically generated feed embodies a set of values about what is important, what is legitimate, and what deserves attention. The question is not whether platforms curate but whether the values embedded in their curation should be subject to democratic accountability.

It would be misleading, however, to press the curator analogy too far. A human curator operates within institutional frameworks that impose constraints — ethical, legal, professional — that have no algorithmic equivalent. More precisely, the scale, speed, and opacity of algorithmic curation produce effects that differ not merely in degree but in kind from anything that human editorial practice has previously encompassed. The information landscape that results is best described, perhaps, not as a curated gallery but as an acoustic environment: one in which the amplification of certain frequencies — outrage, anxiety, tribal identity — has been optimised to maximise the duration of listener engagement, with consequences for democratic deliberation that are, at this stage, difficult to overstate.

📝 Задания к Reading 1 — Анализ метафорической аргументации

1. В первом абзаце: 'The dominant metaphor — the platform as a neutral conduit or marketplace for ideas'. Разберите source и target domains. Какие entailments эта метафора несёт в себе? Как 'a marketplace in which the prices are set... by engagement metrics' обнажает её ограничение?

2. Второй абзац вводит альтернативную метафору: 'curators'. Составьте полный список entailments этой метафоры, которые автор использует (явно или имплицитно). Какое ограничение автор признаёт в третьем абзаце?

3. Третий абзац предлагает третью метафору: 'acoustic environment'. Почему автор переходит от 'gallery' к 'acoustic environment'? Какие новые entailments открывает акустическая метафора, которые галерейная скрывала?

4. Найдите в тексте все конструкции qualifying the image. Для каждой: (a) какая именно часть образа квалифицируется, (b) чем признание ограничения усиливает аргумент.

5. Автор использует три последовательные метафоры (conduit → curator → acoustic environment). Напишите четвёртый абзац (80–90 слов), предлагающий четвёртую метафору — с полным развёртыванием entailments, qualifying и аналитическим выводом.

Слово Значение / Коллокация Пример
